title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: erifunctions Carter Center ERI team’s R package — API Data Analysts (DAs) Epidemiologists (Epis) interact TCC’s Azure-centred data system across countries (Haiti, DR, Uganda, OEPA) diseases (malaria, oncho, LF, SCH, STH). Users domain experts, software developers: install install_github() / renv, authenticate via interactive browser auth, call functions — edit package. Optimise every change clarity reliability.

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: Data lives data/ Azure blob canonical path built eri_data_path(): eri_approve() human gate. Nothing reaches processed/ without ; writes approval log registers file data catalog (_catalog/data_catalog.yaml). projects/ blob legacy contractor (hsp-mal) space; V2 migrating authority data/ (see roadmap Phase 3). Metadata stores (catalog, ODK registry, artifact registry) YAML blobs — see ADR-0002 concurrency rules touching .
code: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_type}/{layer}/   raw/        as-received from source   staged/     DQ-checked, awaiting approval   processed/  analyst-approved, canonical

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: Naming: exported functions eri_* (domain verbs like run_dq_checks); internal helpers .eri_* @keywords internal, exported. Style: cli::cli_* user-facing messages; roxygen2 markdown; functions get @examples (wrap live Azure/ODK calls \dontrun{}). Source layout: one domain per file R/ (dal.R, dq.R, catalog.R, odk*.R, research.R, reports*.R, spatial.R, epi*.R, onboarding.R, …). Schemas bundled YAML inst/schemas/; templates inst/templates/. changing exports roxygen, regenerate NAMESPACE/man/ devtools::document().

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: codebase grew project-driven development, scattered local fixes global problems. adding function pattern: Search existing helper first — reuse azure_io() / eri_read() / eri_write() / eri_data_path() DQ/catalog/research machinery rather re-implementing. problem general (countries/diseases/users hit ), solve generally put decision ADR — don’t bury one-single workflow. Keep roadmap ADRs current: change alters plan decision, update docs/roadmap.md / add ADR PR.

title: CLAUDE.md — erifunctions working memory
text: devtools::document() exports/docs changed; devtools::check() pass clean. devtools::test() — unit tests run offline; live integration tests tests/testthat/test-smoke.R run Sys.setenv(ERI_SMOKE_TESTS = "true"). Update NEWS.md current phase heading bump DESCRIPTION Version shipping. Note: cloud dev container local R/quarto — R CMD check pkgdown::build_site() verified GitHub Actions CI, locally.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: Open issue first. Every change starts GitHub issue scoped ~1-2 hours work. Branch dev {issue-number}-{short-slug} (e.g. 54-quarto-template). PR targets dev, main. main receives version-bumped phase releases. devtools::check() must 0 errors, 0 warnings requesting review. Issues closed manually PR merges.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: Schemas live inst/schemas/ (surveillance) inst/schemas/cmr/ (CMR). Run eri_onboard_country() eri_onboard_cmr() generate template working directory. Fill TODO sections. Run eri_schema_validate("your_schema.yaml") — fix issues reports. Copy finished file inst/schemas/ (inst/schemas/cmr/) open PR.

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: exported functions named eri_*() — verb-first, tab-completable. Internal helpers prefixed . documented #' @keywords internal. Use cli::cli_abort() / cli::cli_inform() / cli::cli_alert_success() user-facing messages — message(), warning(), stop(). non-ASCII characters R source files (em dashes, smart quotes, tab literals). withr::local_mocked_bindings — use testthat::local_mocked_bindings(). Temp files cleaned withr::defer(unlink(tmp)).

title: Contributing to erifunctions
text: issues phase closed: 1. Update NEWS.md README.md. 2. Bump Version: DESCRIPTION. 3. Merge dev → main via squash PR. 4. Create GitHub release tagged v{version}.

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: eri_onboard_disease() writes one YAML file per data type: Use dry_run = TRUE first preview written:
code: library(erifunctions)  eri_onboard_disease(   disease    = "schisto",   country    = "ug",   data_types = c("mda", "prevalence"),   output_dir = "schemas/"     # write locally, not to the package ) # Writes schemas/ug_schisto_programmatic_treatment.yaml and #        schemas/ug_schisto_research_prevalence.yaml (ADR-0012: mda -> programmatic/ #        treatment, prevalence -> research/prevalence) eri_onboard_disease("rb", "eth", dry_run = TRUE)

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Open skeleton file complete TODO sections: Key decisions make column: required – pipeline hard-error column missing? type – numeric, character, categorical, date aliases – column name variants found raw data files range – numeric columns; flag implausible values allowed_values – categorical columns; triggers flag value unexpected translations – map raw variants canonical value (e.g. "YES" -> "Yes")
code: # ug_schisto_programmatic_treatment.yaml (excerpt) columns:   species_target:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [Species, species, schisto_species]     allowed_values:       - S.mansoni        # fill in the species relevant to this country       - S.haematobium    school_name:           # add country-specific columns here     required: false     type: character     aliases: [School, school_name, etablissement]

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Run eri_schema_validate() check structural problems: Fix flagged issues moving . Common problems: Missing required type column definition consistency rule referencing column name defined columns temporal.year_col temporal.period_col pointing column columns
code: result <- eri_schema_validate("schemas/ug_schisto_mda.yaml") # Prints: "Schema ug_schisto_mda.yaml is valid." if no issues found # Or:     a summary of issues with field names and messages print(result)

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: submitting, test schema real synthetic data: sample data passes cleanly (nrow(result$flags) == 0), schema ready real data.
code: schema <- load_dq_schema("ug", "schisto", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) # azcontainer = NULL tells it to use the local file, not Azure  # Create a small synthetic data frame that matches your schema sample_data <- data.frame(   year       = c(2023L, 2023L, 2024L),   round      = c(1L, 2L, 1L),   district   = c("Kampala", "Wakiso", "Kampala"),   target_pop = c(5000L, 3200L, 5100L),   treated    = c(4800L, 3100L, 4950L),   coverage_pct = c(96.0, 96.9, 97.1) )  result <- run_dq_checks(sample_data, schema) cat("Flags:", nrow(result$flags), "\n")

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: Copy validated schema file inst/schemas/ using four-part identity {country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}.yaml (ADR-0012): PR checklist: Schema file inst/schemas/ correct name eri_schema_validate() returns 0 issues load_dq_schema("ug", "schisto", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) works least one load_dq_schema + eri_schema_validate test added tests/testthat/test-onboarding.R following existing pattern NEWS.md entry next version section
code: inst/schemas/ug_schisto_programmatic_treatment.yaml inst/schemas/ug_schisto_research_prevalence.yaml

title: Adding a New NTD Program
text: new program needs custom analytics beyond generic DQ pipeline (e.g. DALY calculations, TAS interpretation, programme status maps), add new file R/epi_{disease}.R following pattern R/epi_lf.R: Export document following standard roxygen conventions, add tests tests/testthat/test-epi-schisto.R.
code: # R/epi_schisto.R  #' Compute school-level Kato-Katz prevalence from survey data #' #' @param data A tibble with one row per tested individual. #' @param result_col `chr` Column containing `"Positive"` / `"Negative"`. #' @param group_cols `chr` vector Grouping columns (school, district, year). #' @returns A tibble of prevalence estimates by group. #' @export eri_schisto_kk_prevalence <- function(data, result_col, group_cols) {   # implementation here }

title: Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams
text: big one: data system . nothing set . Thanks baked-defaults (see ?get_azure_storage_connection ADR-0008), first time command needs Azure browser opens, sign Carter Center account, sign-remembered rest session. storage_name picks blob connect : "data" (governed three-layer data system) "projects" (legacy contractor space). analyst work "data". Confirm works listing top container — get tibble back, : name column holds full path container root (deeper listing like eri_list("uga/demo/research/raw", …) returns uga/demo/research/raw/<file> names); pass full_names = FALSE just leaf filenames. automation / CI (headless). scheduled job browser. Set service principal — ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET environment (pass creds_yaml_path=) — get_azure_storage_connection() signs non-interactively instead. secret true secret ; tenant, app, endpoint non-sensitive defaults. 403 Forbidden? bug — Azure telling account access storage. Access granted administrator (Azure RBAC), package. Ask ERI admin add .
code: # The first call this session opens your browser to sign in. # Nothing prints on success — you get a connection object back. data_con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "data") eri_list("", azcontainer = data_con) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   name      size isdir lastModified #>   <chr>    <dbl> <lgl> <dttm> #> 1 dr          NA TRUE  2026-01-15 09:12:04 #> 2 ht          NA TRUE  2026-02-01 14:30:51 #> 3 uga         NA TRUE  2026-03-22 11:05:18 #> …

what: ODK Central — three lines in your settings file
title: Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams
text: ODK Central signs email password, go .Renviron . Open : Add three lines, save restart R: Now connect — init_odk_connection() reads automatically, password never appears script: Confirm works listing projects can see: Pass con ODK functions con = con. full ODK workflow — registering form, monitoring , pulling submissions — see ODK Central guide. automation / CI. Instead email+password, can reuse bearer token: set ODK_TOKEN environment call ODK functions con = NULL — fall back ODK_URL + ODK_TOKEN.
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() ODK_URL=https://your-odk-server.org/ ODK_USER=you@example.org ODK_PASS=your-password con <- init_odk_connection() #> ✔ Connected to <https://your-odk-server.org/>. Session expires 2026-06-26T20:00:45.690Z. list_odk_projects(con = con) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   project_id project   description #>        <int> <chr>     <chr> #> 1          5 Uganda    NA #> 2          7 Training  NA #> 3         11 testing   NA

title: Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams
text: SharePoint uses browser sign-Azure (via Microsoft365R), supply site URL: Confirm works listing folder site’s document library: reading uploading files, see eri_sharepoint_read() / eri_sharepoint_upload() SharePoint workflow vignette. service-principal path. SharePoint browser-sign-erifunctions — headless/CI path . Use interactively.
code: site <- eri_sharepoint_connect("https://cartercenter.sharepoint.com/sites/ERI") # Browser opens on first use; the token is cached for the session. eri_sharepoint_list(site, "Shared Documents/Malaria/2024") #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   name              size modified            is_folder path #>   <chr>            <dbl> <dttm>              <lgl>     <chr> #> 1 summary.xlsx     20481 2026-04-02 08:15:00 FALSE     Shared Documents/Malaria/2024/summary.xlsx #> 2 maps             NA    2026-03-28 16:40:00 TRUE      Shared Documents/Malaria/2024/maps #> …

title: Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams
text: Teams optional — need want erifunctions post messages (example, eri_notify_dq() posts data-quality summary channel). three ways , easiest first: simplest: incoming webhook. Ask channel owner incoming webhook URL, put .Renviron, can post channel token : direct messages, use Graph API. get_teams_connection() returns token — ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_TOKEN one, otherwise via interactive device-code sign-. Unlike Azure, path zero-config: device-code flow needs ERIFUNCTIONS_APP_ID set .Renviron (without ’ll get “Teams token app ID found” warning). people need post channel, webhook already covers. Confirm works sending DM: automation / CI. webhook (ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_WEBHOOK) robust scheduled jobs — needs interactive sign-. conditional-access policies block device-code flow, webhook way . DMs automation, set pre-obtained ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_TOKEN.
code: ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_WEBHOOK=https://outlook.office.com/webhook/… eri_teams_send(message = "Hello from erifunctions.") #> ℹ Sending Teams message via incoming webhook. token <- get_teams_connection() eri_teams_send(message = "Test message to myself.", to = "self", token = token) #> ℹ Sending Teams DM via Graph API.

title: Connecting to Azure, ODK Central, SharePoint, and Teams
text: settings live one file. Open usethis::edit_r_environ() keep lines actually need: Always restart R editing .Renviron. Everything file read startup — none ever written scripts committed git.
code: # --- Azure storage -------------------------------------------------------- # Optional for analysts: tenant / app / endpoint have working built-in defaults. # ERIFUNCTIONS_TENANT_ID=<Azure tenant ID> # ERIFUNCTIONS_APP_ID=<Azure app registration ID> # ERIFUNCTIONS_RESOURCE_ENDPOINT=<storage account endpoint URL> ERIFUNCTIONS_STORAGE_NAME=projects ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME=data  # Your identity (appears in approval and access logs) ERI_ANALYST_ID=firstname.lastname # Optional: refuse governed actions when ERI_ANALYST_ID is unset (teams / CI) # ERI_REQUIRE_ANALYST_ID=true  # --- ODK Central ---------------------------------------------------------- ODK_URL=https://your-odk-server.org/ ODK_USER=you@example.org ODK_PASS=your-password  # --- Teams (optional) ----------------------------------------------------- # ERIFUNCTIONS_TEAMS_WEBHOOK=https://outlook.office.com/webhook/…  # --- Automation / CI only ------------------------------------------------- # ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID=<service principal client ID> # ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET=<service principal client secret>

Azure access upload / stage / approve steps (zero-config browser sign-— see connections guide). parsing step (eri_ingest_cmr()) offline — just reads local Excel file. note examples . upload, stage, approve steps run live Azure projects/data blobs real, registered countries — outputs shown illustrations rather run . Unlike surveillance ODK guides, throwaway-sandbox path stage/approve: eri_stage_cmr() accepts registered RB-expansion country (rejects made-one). parsing step run real small synthetic example report ships package, output exactly ’ll see.
code: library(erifunctions)

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: filled monthly template lands projects blob, per-country, per-period folder. period six-digit YYYYMM: R eri_upload() — keeps whole monthly run one place one tool. (file already arrived via Azure Storage Explorer SharePoint, ’s fine ; can skip ahead staging.) Connect projects blob upload file path:
code: projects: health-rb-country-expansion-dev/raw/filled_templates/{country}/{period}/{file}.xlsx e.g.      …/raw/filled_templates/uga/202406/uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx # Connect to the projects blob explicitly (the same blob eri_stage_cmr reads from). projects_con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "projects")  eri_upload(   "uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx",   "health-rb-country-expansion-dev/raw/filled_templates/uga/202406/uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx",   azcontainer = projects_con ) # (uploads the file; no console output)

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: Every CMR sheet shape — rows human-readable headers, field-code row, data: row 5 makes whole thing work, ’ll see §3.

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: eri_stage_cmr() pulls filed Excel(s) projects blob copies governed data blob’s staged/ layer, ready review. Give country period: Omit period stages recent one , says : (CMR data lives rblf disease folder — RB onchocerciasis, LF lymphatic filariasis, two programmes reports cover. registered RB-expansion countries — eth, nga, sdn, ssd, uga, mad, tcd — can staged.) Heads-rblf coordinate. CMR always uses disease = "rblf" — combined RB + LF programme code — paths {country}/rblf/cmr/… approve eri_approve(country, "rblf", "cmr", period). Scaffolding new country’s CMR eri_onboard_cmr(create_dirs = TRUE) creates {country}/rblf/cmr/ folders. rblf/cmr per-disease. rblf/cmr interim coordinates: source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), CMR format programmatic channel, eri_split_cmr() () routes sheet disease measure — e.g. RB Treatment → {country}/oncho/programmatic/treatment/. approve disease/measure, one combined rblf/cmr bucket.
code: eri_stage_cmr("uga", "202406") #> ✔ Staged: uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx #> ── ✔ Staged CMR to data blob ────────────────────────────── #> Files: 1 #> Location: uga/rblf/cmr/staged #> ℹ Operation log: uga/rblf/cmr/logs/20240705_141500_eri_stage_cmr_202406.yaml eri_stage_cmr("uga") #> ℹ No period specified; staging most recent: 202406 #> ✔ Staged: uga_cmr_2024_06.xlsx #> …

title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: Now offline heart job. First, see sheets country’s template : (real monthly file many sheets — treatments, MMDP, various trainings, surveys. bundled cmr-example.xlsx small two-sheet subset, RB Treatment + SCH Treatment, can run parse split offline.) read sheet eri_ingest_cmr(). reads row 5 (field codes), keeps #-coded columns, drops template’s blank spacer rows, — pass country — tags row . (use package’s bundled synthetic example ; real work ’d point staged file.) sheet programme, parsed way: column names field codes — stable identifiers can rely across every monthly file. moment eyeball numbers (treated vs target, missing districts) sign . codes, language. field codes (#rbtrt_…) language-neutral, eri_ingest_cmr() parses French template . French file, sheet named French (e.g. "Oncho Traitement") — pass canonical slug country, schema’s sheet_aliases resolves :
code: schema <- load_cmr_schema("uga") names(schema$sheets) #>  [1] "RB Treatment"                  "SCH Treatment" #>  [3] "LF MMDP"                       "VHT Training" #>  [5] "Parish Supervisors Training"   "Local Leaders Training" #>  [7] "Subcounty Supervisor Training" "MMDP (surgery) Training" #>  [9] "MMDP (patient) Training"       "Field Ento Training" #> [11] "Lab Training"                  "LF Surveys" #> [13] "RB Epi Surveys"                "RB Ento Surveys" report <- system.file("extdata", "cmr-example.xlsx", package = "erifunctions")  rb <- eri_ingest_cmr(report, sheet = "RB Treatment", country = "uga") #> ✔ CMR sheet "RB Treatment": 3 data rows, 6 field codes.  rb #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   country `#rbtrt_year` `#rbtrt_month` `#rbtrt_adm1` `#rbtrt_adm2` `#rbtrt_target` `#rbtrt_treated` #>   <chr>   <chr>         <chr>          <chr>         <chr>         <chr>           <chr> #> 1 uga     2024          06             Central       Kampala       12000           11400 #> 2 uga     2024          06             Western       Mbarara       8000            7600 #> 3 uga     2024          06             Eastern       Soroti        6000            5800 sch <- eri_ingest_cmr(report, sheet = "SCH Treatment", country = "uga") #> ✔ CMR sheet "SCH Treatment": 2 data rows, 6 field codes. eri_ingest_cmr("tcd_cmr_2024_06.xlsx", sheet = "rb_treatment", country = "tcd") #> ✔ CMR sheet "Oncho Traitement": 24 data rows, 6 field codes.

what: 4. Split it by disease and measure
title: Uploading and processing a monthly country report (CMR) (for data analysts)
text: sheets per-programme, canonical store per-disease. eri_split_cmr() reads every routable sheet routes rows {country}/{disease}/programmatic/{measure}/staged/ — disease sheet (RB Treatment → oncho, SCH Treatment → sch, LF MMDP → lf), measure category. reads Excel directly (bundled example; real work file uploaded), independently rblf/cmr/staged/ copy eri_stage_cmr() made — copy raw archive; parse + route. Preview routing first dry_run: sheet schema routes workbook doesn’t contain skipped warning — expected , since example two-sheet subset. (none routable sheets present, eri_split_cmr() errors instead silently nothing.) sheet, row? sheet carries per-row #…_disease field whose values programme-coverage codes (RB / RBLF / RBLFSCH — programmes run location), single disease. eri_split_cmr() keeps field data column takes routing disease sheet, treatment numbers never duplicated across diseases. Cross-programme Training sheets, serve programmes , route together combined rblf disease. stage real — one parquet per sheet, disease/measure folder:
code: eri_split_cmr(report, "uga", dry_run = TRUE) #> ✔ CMR sheet "RB Treatment": 3 data rows, 6 field codes. #> ✔ CMR sheet "SCH Treatment": 2 data rows, 6 field codes. #> ℹ Dry run -- nothing written. Routing plan: #>   "RB Treatment"  -> uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_rb_treatment.parquet (3 rows) #>   "SCH Treatment" -> uga/sch/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_sch_treatment.parquet (2 rows) #> Warning: Sheet "LF MMDP" not found in cmr-example.xlsx; skipping.   # ...and the #> Warning: Sheet "VHT Training" not found in cmr-example.xlsx; skipping.  # other #> # ... (the example has only the two treatment sheets; a real file routes them all) eri_split_cmr(report, "uga") #> ✔ "RB Treatment"  -> uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_rb_treatment.parquet #> ✔ "SCH Treatment" -> uga/sch/programmatic/treatment/staged/cmr-example_sch_treatment.parquet #> ── ✔ Split CMR by disease/measure ─────────────────────── #> Sheets: 2 routed #> Diseases: oncho, sch

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: Data moves three layers, nothing becomes official without sign-. Every dataset travels raw/ → staged/ → processed/. raw/ file exactly arrived. staged/ cleaned, quality-checked version awaiting review. processed/ canonical data whole team builds — file gets call eri_approve(). approval human gate.

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: need two things, : R RStudio installed laptop. package, installed Carter Center repository: Azure access needs configuration. first time command needs Azure, browser opens sign Carter Center account; sign-remembered rest session. (keys, environment variables — see ?get_azure_storage_connection.) erifunctions narrates . Load leave verbosity default "full" walkthrough — step--step output shown blocks :
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions") library(erifunctions)  # The outputs shown in this guide are at the default "full" verbosity. # Once you know the steps, eri_verbosity("quiet") trims them to headline # results, warnings, and errors only.

what: 1. Connect to the data blob and name your sandbox
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: surveillance pipeline lives data blob. reading writing functions (eri_read(), eri_write(), eri_upload()) talk default connection, make one pointed data pass explicitly everywhere — way every step targets right place: Every dataset identified five coordinates (ADR-0012): country, disease, data_source (channel — data arrives), data_type (measure — captures), layer. key idea channel separate measure: surveillance data can case line-list one country aggregate weekly count another. extract line-list — one row per case — measure case. Never hand-type folder paths — let eri_data_path() build canonical path , every step agrees things go: data_source data_type extensible — eri_data_model() lists known values, unregistered one warns rather blocks — layer one "raw", "staged", "processed". Country disease free text, exactly can stand atlantis sandbox next real countries without disturbing . Create two folders write (third, processed/, created us approval):
code: data_con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "data") # Our make-believe country and its malaria surveillance data. country     <- "atlantis" disease     <- "malaria" data_source <- "surveillance"   # the channel data_type   <- "case"           # the measure (one row per case)  eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw") #> [1] "atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/raw"  eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged") #> [1] "atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/staged" eri_dir_create(eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw"),    azcontainer = data_con) eri_dir_create(eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged"), azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Directory created!

what: 2. Simulate an as-received extract and store it in raw/
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: real life surveillance officer emails spreadsheet. make one — tiny line-list malaria cases, one row per case. Notice deliberately little messy, way real data always : sex values Spanish (Masculino/Femenino/M), couple district names mis-cased use old spelling (coralia, Nerei City): first thing file preserve untouched, always record actually arrived. Write raw/: raw/ archive source. work toward staged/ — original safe ever need start .
code: extract1 <- data.frame(   Year     = rep(2024, 10),   EpiWeek  = 1:10,   District = c("Coralia", "Nerei", "coralia", "Tritonis", "Marisa",                "Nerei City", "Coralia", "Nerei", "Tritonis", "Marisa"),   Sex      = c("Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Femenino", "M",                "Female", "Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Male"),   Age      = c(34, 27, 5, 60, 41, 19, 73, 8, 52, 30),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) head(extract1) #>   Year EpiWeek   District       Sex Age #> 1 2024       1    Coralia      Male  34 #> 2 2024       2      Nerei    Female  27 #> 3 2024       3    coralia Masculino   5 #> 4 2024       4   Tritonis  Femenino  60 #> 5 2024       5     Marisa         M  41 #> 6 2024       6 Nerei City    Female  19 raw_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw",                            "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.csv") eri_write(extract1, raw_path1, azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: erifunctions know Masculino become Male, Age 250? tell , , DQ schema — small YAML file describing column: type, allowed range allowed values, known fixes, translations. schema contract every future extract dataset checked . Open text editor save following atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml working directory — filename four-part identity {country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}, load_dq_schema() finds . (intentionally short — real schema like dr_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml columns, shape exactly .) One source, many measures. stood space onboarding guide, scaffold defaults aggregate measure (weekly counts) — atlantis_malaria_surveillance_aggregate.yaml. extract line-list, author case measure alongside . whole point separating channel measure: surveillance source can hold case aggregate data. Run eri_data_model() time see known measures. Read slowly — block rule: distinction matters: corrections translations applied recorded; flags problems can resolve. Now publish schema Azure load_dq_schema() can find . looks schemas/{country}_{disease}_{data_source}_{data_type}.yaml, filename matters:
code: country: atlantis disease: malaria data_source: surveillance data_type: case language: en time_grain: case  temporal:   year_col: Year   period_col: EpiWeek  preprocessing:   - remove_smart_quotes   - drop_rows_missing_year  columns:   Year:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [year, YEAR, Año]     range: [2000, 2035]    EpiWeek:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [week, Week, Semana]     range: [1, 53]    District:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [district, Distrito]     allowed_values: [Coralia, Nerei, Tritonis, Marisa]     corrections:       coralia: Coralia       CORALIA: Coralia       "Nerei City": Nerei    Sex:     required: true     type: categorical     aliases: [sex, SEX, Sexo]     allowed_values: [Male, Female]     translations:       Masculino: Male       Femenino: Female       M: Male       F: Female    Age:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [age, AGE, Edad]     range: [0, 120] eri_upload("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml",            "schemas/atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml", azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: Load raw file back raw/, load schema, run checks. run_dq_checks() applies every rule schema hands back cleaned data plus two ledgers — fixed, wants look : Print result full report: Six things fixed automatically nothing needs review — clean extract. can always see exactly changed $log ledger: cleaned data lives result1$data — Spanish sexes mapped Male/Female, district names canonicalised. stage.
code: raw1    <- eri_read(raw_path1, azcontainer = data_con) schema  <- load_dq_schema("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", "case", azcontainer = data_con)  result1 <- run_dq_checks(raw1, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 6 corrections, 0 flags for review. result1 #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 10 rows x 5 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (6 total) ── #> • "Sex": 4 corrections (translation) #> • "District": 2 corrections (correction) #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (0 total) ── #> ✔ No flags. result1$log #> # A tibble: 6 × 6 #>     row column   original_value corrected_value rule        action #>   <int> <chr>    <chr>          <chr>           <chr>       <chr> #> 1     3 Sex      Masculino      Male            translation corrected #> 2     9 Sex      Masculino      Male            translation corrected #> 3     4 Sex      Femenino       Female          translation corrected #> 4     5 Sex      M              Male            translation corrected #> 5     3 District coralia        Coralia         correction  corrected #> 6     6 District Nerei City     Nerei           correction  corrected

what: 5. Write the cleaned data to staged/
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: staged/ holds DQ-checked data awaiting approval. store parquet (compact, typed, fast). Give file name contains reporting period — use period approve next step: Nothing official yet. data checked parked, appear canonical sign .
code: staged_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged",                               "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet") eri_write(result1$data, staged_path1, azcontainer = data_con)

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: gate. eri_approve() finds every staged file whose name contains period give , moves processed/, writes human-readable approval log (approved , ), registers file data catalog teammates can discover : Passing data_type records measure catalog entry. (Omit eri_approve() files data channel level data_type = NA, says — fine channel-data, line-list case.) catalog team’s index canonical data. Confirm dataset now : read canonical file straight back, exactly teammate : whole loop: raw → DQ → staged → approved.
code: eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, "2024-01", data_type = data_type, azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: registered atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet. #> ✔ Approved: atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet #> ✔ Approval log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/2024-01_approval_log.yaml #> ── ✔ Approved "2024-01" ───────────────────────────────── #> Dataset: atlantis / malaria / surveillance / case #> Files: 1 moved to processed #> Approver: your.name #> Location: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20240115_142233_eri_approve_2024-01.yaml eri_catalog_query(country = "atlantis", data_con = data_con) #> # A tibble: 1 × 13 #>   path                        country disease data_source data_type layer  period  … #>   <chr>                       <chr>   <chr>   <chr>       <chr>     <chr>  <chr> #> 1 atlantis/malaria/surveill…  atlantis malaria surveillance case      proce… 2024-01 processed_path1 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "processed",                                  "atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet") official <- eri_read(processed_path1, azcontainer = data_con) nrow(official)   # 10 approved cases

what: 7. A second extract arrives — this time with errors
title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: next reporting file lands, real data rarely clean. one two genuine problems analyst must judge: Age 250 (impossible), district Atlantica one four (typo, new district schema know yet): Archive raw/, run checks: time two flags. schema fixed safely , guess age 250 unknown district — call. Look exactly rows: investigate (call surveillance officer, check source form) decide: age data-entry slip 25, Atlantica really Marisa. Fix cleaned data, re-run checks confirm clean bill health: just edit schema? Atlantica real new district, right fix add allowed_values re-upload schema — every future extract accepts . Use correction/translation genuine fixes; use flag--edit one-data errors like impossible age. Now stage approve corrected second period, exactly : now two approved periods catalog atlantis, approval log recording signed . Going : cross-period anomalies. Range allowed-value checks catch bad cells. catch suspicious trend — district whose case count suddenly triples — see add_anomaly_pct_change(), can chain onto run_dq_checks() result several periods stacked together.
code: extract2 <- data.frame(   Year     = rep(2024, 10),   EpiWeek  = 11:20,   District = c("Coralia", "Atlantica", "Nerei", "Tritonis", "Marisa",                "coralia", "Nerei", "Coralia", "Tritonis", "Marisa"),   Sex      = c("Male", "Female", "Masculino", "Female", "M",                "Female", "Male", "Femenino", "Male", "Female"),   Age      = c(34, 27, 250, 60, 41, 19, 73, 8, 52, 30),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) raw_path2 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "raw",                            "atlantis_malaria_2024-02.csv") eri_write(extract2, raw_path2, azcontainer = data_con)  raw2    <- eri_read(raw_path2, azcontainer = data_con) result2 <- run_dq_checks(raw2, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 4 corrections, 2 flags for review.  result2 #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 10 rows x 5 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (4 total) ── #> • "Sex": 3 corrections (translation) #> • "District": 1 correction (correction) #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (2 total) ── #> ! Value not in allowed_values list: 1 row [District] (e.g. Atlantica (row 2)) #> ! Value outside expected range [0, 120]: 1 row [Age] (e.g. 250 (row 3)) #> #> ℹ See `result$flags` for the full row-level detail. result2$flags #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>     row column   value     issue #>   <int> <chr>    <chr>     <chr> #> 1     3 Age      250       Value outside expected range [0, 120] #> 2     2 District Atlantica Value not in allowed_values list fixed <- result2$data fixed$Age[3]      <- 25 fixed$District[2] <- "Marisa"  result2b <- run_dq_checks(fixed, schema) #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 0 corrections, 0 flags for review. staged_path2 <- eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, "staged",                               "atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet") eri_write(result2b$data, staged_path2, azcontainer = data_con)  eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, "2024-02", data_type = data_type, azcontainer = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: registered atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet. #> ✔ Approved: atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet #> ── ✔ Approved "2024-02" ───────────────────────────────── #> Dataset: atlantis / malaria / surveillance / case #> Files: 1 moved to processed #> Approver: your.name #> Location: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed

title: Ingesting a surveillance dataset: raw to approved (for data analysts)
text: ran practice, remove everything created leave trace. deletes whole atlantis sandbox (three layers logs), schema, catalog entries: Confirm nothing remains, delete local files: delete freely : Atlantis invented data fake. Real Carter Center surveillance data different — approved country data must never deleted casually, copied Azure, committed git. discipline guide builds — raw kept untouched, DQ staging, human approval gate anything becomes canonical — exactly keeps protected data trustworthy. doubt, ask delete.
code: # Delete the entire sandbox namespace (raw + staged + processed + logs). # eri_dir_delete() removes a non-empty directory recursively in one call; using # the erifunctions helpers (not raw AzureStor) keeps every step in the package. eri_dir_delete("atlantis", azcontainer = data_con)  # Delete the schema you published. eri_delete("schemas/atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml", azcontainer = data_con)  # Remove the two catalog entries (the catalog is shared, so tidy up after yourself). eri_catalog_remove("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet",                    data_con = data_con) eri_catalog_remove("atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/processed/atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet",                    data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Catalog: removed atlantis_malaria_2024-01.parquet. #> ✔ Catalog: removed atlantis_malaria_2024-02.parquet. eri_catalog_query(country = "atlantis", data_con = data_con) #> No catalog entries match the specified filters.  unlink("atlantis_malaria_surveillance_case.yaml")   # the local schema file

One call — eri_ingest() — read--DQ--stage steps (§2, §4, §5) single shot, data including sandbox just built (takes country / disease / data_source / data_type using). step hand see every layer; comfortable, eri_ingest() fast path, eri_approve() — sign-— always human gate end. (legacy hsp-mal cutover, passing mirror_pipeline = "hsp-mal" also mirrors output old projects blob — default.) one planned set short, task-oriented guides — one common job analyst epidemiologist . title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Nothing fails silently, nothing stays open accident. Operations write structured logs go; data-quality flags persisted eri_dq_log() (eri_ingest() ). eri_logs() turns pile triage list, eri_logs_resolve() marks item handled — handled — drops open backlog without ever erasing record.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Install package sign Azure (zero-config browser; see connections guide).
code: library(erifunctions)

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Two kinds log end {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/[{data_type}/]logs/ (measure level present data approved one — eri_logs() reads layouts): Operation logs — written automatically eri_ingest(), eri_approve(), eri_stage(), eri_odk_sync(), friends. records operation, ran , parameters, — failed — error. Data-quality logs — $flags run_dq_checks() R session persist . eri_dq_log() writes place (eri_ingest() calls automatically), data-quality issue someone hit last month still discoverable today. Let’s make one sandbox.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Picking ingest guide, run_dq_checks() result couple flags. Persist :
code: # result <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema)   # from the ingest guide # Pass the same measure you approve with (here "case") so the DQ log co-locates # with the approval log under the measure level. eri_dq_log(result, "atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", data_type = "case", period = "2024-01") #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_093000_dq_flags_2024-01.yaml #> ✔ Logged 2 DQ flags (needs_review).

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: Now common slip: try approve period file staged. errors — leaves error log behind:
code: eri_approve("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", "2024-02", data_type = "case") #> ℹ Operation log: atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/logs/20260626_100000_eri_approve_2024-02.yaml #> Error: No staged files found matching "2024-02" in atlantis/malaria/surveillance/case/staged.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: eri_logs() reads logs hands triage list, newest first. Scope dataset (fast), call bare system-wide view: (tibble also carries log_path, n_issues, handled* columns — shown trimmed .) Filter just failures: status = "needs_review" narrows data-quality items instead; operation =, analyst =, since = filter rest. tibble also carries data_source (channel) data_type (measure, data approved one). Leaving country/disease/data_source/data_type scans whole data blob system-wide backlog (scoping faster).
code: eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance") #> 2 logs (2 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 2 × 15 #>   timestamp            operation   status       period  summary           analyst … #>   <chr>                <chr>       <chr>        <chr>   <chr>             <chr> #> 1 2026-06-26T10:00:00Z eri_approve error        2024-02 No staged files…  da.one #> 2 2026-06-26T09:30:00Z dq_flags    needs_review 2024-01 2 flags           da.one eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", status = "error") #> 1 log (1 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   operation   status period  summary #>   <chr>       <chr>  <chr>   <chr> #> 1 eri_approve error  2024-02 No staged files found matching '2024-02'.

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: investigate failure, stage file, re-approve successfully. Now close original error leaves open backlog — note whoever reads next: Re-list, error gone open backlog — data-quality item remains: Resolving never deletes log — adds triage record. Pass include_handled = TRUE see closed item, handled note:
code: backlog <- eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance", status = "error")  eri_logs_resolve(   backlog$log_path[1],   note = "Staged the file, then re-approved 2024-02." ) #> ✔ Marked 20260626_100000_eri_approve_2024-02.yaml handled. eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance") #> 1 log (1 needing attention). #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   operation status       period  summary #>   <chr>     <chr>        <chr>   <chr> #> 1 dq_flags  needs_review 2024-01 2 flags eri_logs("atlantis", "malaria", "surveillance",          status = "error", include_handled = TRUE)[, c("operation", "handled", "handled_by", "handled_at")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   operation   handled handled_by handled_at #>   <chr>       <lgl>   <chr>      <chr> #> 1 eri_approve TRUE    da.one     2026-06-26T10:15:00Z

title: Triaging the error & data-quality log backlog (for data analysts)
text: point shared backlog. logs live Azure, anyone’s laptop — analyst away something needs attention, colleague can run one command see whole picture: see failures, error messages, — anything already handled — dealt note left. “going DR upload last week?” guesswork.
code: # Anyone on the team, across every dataset: eri_logs(status = "error")

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: ODK Central front door; erifunctions brings data one governed pipeline. register form (registry team’s record forms tracked), sync submissions {country}/{disease}/research/raw/, flows raw → staged → processed lifecycle — eri_approve() human gate — every dataset. ODK lands. source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), ODK research channel’s collection format (format: odk), eri_odk_sync() writes research source. measure (tas, prevalence, …) optional assigned later, clean form final dataset — paths carry measure level yet.

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: R RStudio installed. package: ODK Central credentials. Store .Renviron never end scripts. Open usethis::edit_r_environ(), add three lines, save restart R: Everything reads automatically — never type password script. Azure access zero-config: first command needs opens browser sign (see ?get_azure_storage_connection). One comfort setting — leave verbosity default "full" walkthrough (step--step output shown ):
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions") ODK_URL=https://your-odk-server.org/ ODK_USER=you@example.org ODK_PASS=your-password library(erifunctions) # eri_verbosity("quiet")   # later, to trim to headlines + warnings only

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Heads — first step happens browser, R. Creating test project, uploading form, submitting practice entries done ODK Central web interface. erifunctions takes Connect () data server.

what: Put a form on the server
title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)

Find machine upload test project ODK Central: ODK Central web interface: open (create) test project ▸ New ▸ Form ▸ upload .xlsx ▸ Publish. submit 2–3 fake entries: form page click Submit (Enketo web form), fill made-values, send. Now data work .

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)

code: list_odk_projects(con = con) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   project_id project   description #>        <int> <chr>     <chr> #> 1          5 Uganda    NA #> 2          7 Training  NA #> 3         11 testing   NA            # ← your sandbox project (your ids will differ) list_odk_forms(con = con, project_id = 11) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   xmlFormId                  name #>   <chr>                      <chr> #> 1 eri_test_river_prospection ERI Test — River Prospection project_id <- 11 form_id    <- "eri_test_river_prospection"

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: eri_survey_status() --glance health check — many submissions, recent one, recent activity: prints one-line summary, really tibble — store see full metrics (7- 30-day counts, open/closed state): Called just project_id reports every form project; neither argument, every form every project can see — handy Monday-morning sweep across surveys.
code: eri_survey_status(project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id, con = con) #> ── Survey Status (1 form) ────────────────────────────── #> • eri_test_river_prospection [open] - 3 total, last: 2026-06-25T19:57:33.690Z st <- eri_survey_status(project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id, con = con) as.data.frame(st) #>   project_id project_name                    form_id                    form_name           server_url status total_submissions   last_submission_at submissions_7d submissions_30d #> 1         11      testing eri_test_river_prospection ERI Test — River Prospection https://your-odk… open                 3 2026-06-25T19:57:33.690Z              3               3

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Field staff collect app users — per-project data-collection accounts. can create one assign form straight R. (calls change live project; remove demo user Clean .) Check form’s assignments — new collector listed:
code: # Create an app user (a data collector) in the project. new_user <- update_odk_app_user_role(   action     = "create",   con        = con,   project_id = project_id,   actor_name = "Demo Collector" ) new_user #> $actor_name #> [1] "Demo Collector" #> $actor_id #> [1] 250 #> $project_id #> [1] 11 # Give that user access to our form. role_id 2 is ODK's "App User" data-collection role. update_odk_app_user_role(   action     = "assign",   con        = con,   project_id = project_id,   form_id    = form_id,   role_id    = 2,   actor_id   = new_user$actor_id ) #> [1] TRUE list_odk_form_users(con = con, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id)

what: Managing a whole team at once
title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Don’t click 40 collectors one time. Put actions CSV columns project_id, form_id, action, actor_name (action create, assign, remove) hand eri_odk_bulk_users(). Always run dry_run = TRUE first — validates every row live server reports problems together changing anything:
code: eri_odk_bulk_users("collectors.csv", con = con, dry_run = TRUE) #> ℹ Fetching project/form metadata for pre-flight... #> ✔ Pre-flight passed. 2 rows to process. #> ℹ Dry run -- no changes will be made. #>   [1] assign "Jane Fieldworker" on eri_test_river_prospection (project 11) #>   [2] create "John Fieldworker" on eri_test_river_prospection (project 11)

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: form deliberately simple — one row per submission, one table. real ODK forms repeat groups: section enumerator fills per submission — several larvae sampled one site, several household members one visit, several nets given one household. ODK Central exports repeat group table, form one repeat comes two tables: parent table — one row per submission (named {form_id}), child table — one row per repeat instance (named {form_id}-{repeat_name}), linked back parent PARENT_KEY column whose value matches parent row’s KEY. erifunctions captures — nothing silently dropped.

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Everything far moved data ODK Central. Sometimes need direction: stack records already exist — collected paper, living old spreadsheet — want land ODK Central sit alongside field submissions flow pipeline. eri_odk_upload() exactly : reads table creates one submission per row existing published form. mapping mirror image download: columns matched form fields name, using flattening saw — field /data/visit/date column visit-date, repeat groups supplied {form_id}-{repeat} child tables linked PARENT_KEY. download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) result valid input — download upload round-trip. Say historical river-prospection records backfill. Build (read) table whose columns match form’s fields: Always dry-run first. dry_run = TRUE validates table live form — unknown columns, required fields, value types (dates, numbers, geopoints), select-value choice lists — sends nothing: non-empty tibble tells exactly cell fix (e.g. river_stage value "purple" → “value(s) choice list”). clean, drop dry_run send : instanceID submission derived deterministically key_col (record_id). makes upload safe re-run: second time, ODK Central recognises ids rejects , nothing duplicated — — fix couple rows run , change rest skip. bad row never aborts batch: comes back failed (server’s message) neighbours load. Forms repeats. Pass named-list shape got download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) — parent table first, {form_id}-{repeat} child table PARENT_KEY column linking parent’s KEY. eri_odk_upload() rebuilds nested submission attaches repeat right parent. Two limits know. form must published (can’t backfill draft), attachments can’t sent creation — ODK Central API constraint, photos/GPS traces scope upload.
code: backfill <- data.frame(   site_name       = c("Old Ford", "Bend Camp"),   prospection_date = c("2025-11-03", "2025-11-04"),   river_stage     = c("low", "medium"),   blackfly_count  = c(12L, 7L),   collector       = c("paper-archive", "paper-archive"),   record_id       = c("hist-001", "hist-002")    # a stable key we'll hash for the instanceID ) eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id", dry_run = TRUE) #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ℹ `dry_run` is on -- no submissions were sent. #> # A tibble: 0 × 4 #> # ℹ 4 variables: table <chr>, column <chr>, row <int>, issue <chr> eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id") #> ✔ Validation clean: all columns map to form fields. #> ✔ Uploaded to "eri_test_river_prospection": 2 created, 0 already present. #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   instance_id     status  http_status message #>   <chr>           <chr>         <int> <chr> #> 1 uuid:9f2c1a…    created         201 NA #> 2 uuid:4a7b86…    created         201 NA eri_odk_upload(backfill, project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,                con = con, key_col = "record_id") #> ✔ Uploaded to "eri_test_river_prospection": 0 created, 2 already present.

title: Working with ODK Central: connect, monitor, and pull form data (for data analysts)
text: Practice run done — remove everything created leave trace. Finally, ODK Central web interface, delete test form (Form ▸ ⋯ ▸ Delete) test project want gone . Deregister vs purge. real form, eri_odk_deregister() soft-deletes — flips registry entry active: false rather erasing , record tracked (sync history) survives audit; inactive entry harmless won’t show eri_odk_list_registered(). used eri_odk_purge() sandbox: hard-deletes entry (active already-inactive) shared registry left exactly found . delete freely : form submissions invented. Real ODK forms field submissions never deleted casually — primary record work done field. discipline guide builds — register, sync raw/, quality-check, approve human gate — keeps real data trustworthy.
code: # Remove the demo collector from ODK Central (revoke its form access, then delete the account). update_odk_app_user_role(action = "revoke", con = con, project_id = project_id,                          form_id = form_id, role_id = 2, actor_id = new_user$actor_id) update_odk_app_user_role(action = "delete", con = con, project_id = project_id,                          actor_id = new_user$actor_id) # This was a sandbox, so PURGE the registry entry (hard-delete) to leave no trace. # For a real form you'd use eri_odk_deregister() instead, which soft-deletes # (active: false) and keeps the sync history for audit. eri_odk_purge(project_id = project_id, form_id = form_id,               server_url = "https://your-odk-server.org/", data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Purged 1 registry entry for "eri_test_river_prospection" (project 11).  # If you also tried the repeat form in section 4, purge it too. eri_odk_purge(project_id = project_id, form_id = "eri_test_river_repeat",               server_url = "https://your-odk-server.org/", data_con = data_con) #> ✔ Purged 1 registry entry for "eri_test_river_repeat" (project 11). # Delete the whole uga/demo sandbox namespace (raw + staged + processed + logs). # eri_dir_delete() removes it recursively — the in-package path, no Storage Explorer. eri_dir_delete("uga/demo", azcontainer = data_con)

code: country: atlantis disease: malaria data_source: surveillance data_type: aggregate language: en  admin:   admin1_col: Admin1   admin2_col: Admin2   admin1_spatial: spatial/atlantis/atlantis_admin1.shp   admin2_spatial: spatial/atlantis/atlantis_admin2.shp  temporal:   year_col: Year   period_col: EpiWeek   max_gap: 2  preprocessing:   - remove_smart_quotes   - drop_rows_missing_year  columns:   Year:     required: true     type: numeric     range: [2000, 2035]   EpiWeek:     required: true     type: numeric     range: [1, 53]   Admin1:     required: true     type: categorical     # TODO: add allowed_values (list of valid admin1 names)   Admin2:     required: true     type: character    # TODO: add your disease-specific columns here   # CasesConfirmed:   #   required: false   #   type: numeric   #   range: [0, 1000000]  consistency:   # TODO: add cross-field rules here

text: eri_schema_validate() checks structure — required sections, valid column types, temporal/consistency rules reference columns actually exist. Run file just edited: empty tibble means ’s valid. something , get row per problem. Say fat- finger two column types numbr: Fix schema re-run get clean ✔. green light use .

Valid: numeric, character, categorical, date. #> ✖ Column 'EpiWeek' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, character, categorical, date.  # The returned tibble pinpoints each issue: #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   issue_type    field                message #>   <chr>         <chr>                <chr> #> 1 invalid_value columns.Year.type    Column 'Year' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, … #> 2 invalid_value columns.EpiWeek.type Column 'EpiWeek' has invalid type 'numbr'. Valid: numeric, …

title: Onboarding a new country, disease, or data type (for data analysts)
text: Case Management Reports (CMR) monthly Excel returns country programs. Onboarding one writes CMR schema template (pass create_dirs = TRUE) staging folders combined rblf disease code (RB + LF — programmes reports cover), eri_stage_cmr() lands raw Excel. Dry-run first: rblf/cmr staging archive, canonical home. source ≠ measure model (ADR-0012), CMR format programmatic channel. eri_split_cmr() reads staged Excel routes sheet disease measure — e.g. RB Treatment → {country}/oncho/programmatic/treatment/, approve disease/measure. rblf/cmr/ transitional landing/archive; canonical, approved data per-disease (shared sheets like Training route together combined rblf disease). See CMR guide full upload → stage → split → approve flow. real: CMR template ships common report sheets commented — uncomment ones country files, make sheet’s field_code_prefix match #tag_ row Excel file. test eri_ingest_cmr().
code: eri_onboard_cmr("atlantis", "Atlantis", create_dirs = TRUE, dry_run = TRUE) #> ℹ Dry run -- nothing will be written or created. #>   Would write: atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/raw/ #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/staged/ #>     Would create: atlantis/rblf/cmr/processed/ eri_onboard_cmr("atlantis", "Atlantis", create_dirs = TRUE) #> ✔ CMR schema template written to atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml. #> ✔ CMR Azure directories created under atlantis/rblf/cmr/. #> ℹ Next steps: #>   1. Open atlantis_cmr_schema.yaml and uncomment the sheets your country uses. #>   2. Match field_code_prefix to the #tag_ row in your CMR Excel file. #>   3. Submit via pull request to: inst/schemas/cmr/atlantis.yaml #>   4. Test ingestion with eri_ingest_cmr('your_file.xlsx', country = 'atlantis').

title: Data Quality Pipeline
code: library(erifunctions)  # Load the bundled DR malaria case schema (falls back to local if Azure unavailable) schema <- load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria_case", azcontainer = NULL)  # Run all automated checks result <- run_dq_checks(raw_data, schema)  # Inspect result                # calls dq_report() automatically

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: schema YAML file inst/schemas/ (optionally Azure schemas/). key blocks :
code: country: dr disease: malaria_case version: "1.0"  preprocessing:   - remove_smart_quotes      # fix curly quotes from Excel   - strip_column_name_spaces # remove spaces/parens from header row  temporal:   date_col:  sample_date   year_col:  year   cross_check_year_col: year  # flags rows where year != year(sample_date)  columns:   year:     required: true     type: numeric     aliases: [Year, Año, ano]      # recognised alternative column names     range: [2000, 2035]    province:     required: true     type: character     aliases: [Province_Residence, Provincia_residencia]     allowed_values: [Distrito Nacional, Santo Domingo, ...]     translations:                  # auto-corrected silently       "sto. Domingo": "Santo Domingo"     corrections:                   # also auto-corrected       "Sto Domingo": "Santo Domingo"  derived:   imported_flag:     formula: "as.integer(province %in% c('Haiti', 'Extranjero', 'Otros'))"  consistency:   epiweek_valid:     lhs: epiweek     op: ">="     rhs_value: 1     message: "Epiweek below 1"   positives_le_tested:     lhs: n_positive     op: "<="     rhs: n_tested     message: "Positives exceed tested"

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: pipeline runs steps order: Preprocessing — removes smart quotes, optionally strips column name whitespace, drops rows missing year value. Alias resolution — renames recognised alternative column headers canonical name defined schema. Required-column check — adds NA-row flag column marked required: true completely absent data. Type coercion — converts columns numeric, date, character declared; flags values coerced. Range checks — flags numeric values outside [min, max] range. Translations corrections — applies lookup maps silently, logging change $log. Allowed-values check — flags values allowed_values list. NA fill — fills NA na_fill-annotated columns specified default logs change. Temporal cross-check — flags rows year(date_col) != year_col. Derived columns — evaluates formula (data, ...) adds result new column. Aggregate consistency — derived column, checks formula existing value flags discrepancies (useful pre-computed totals).

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: $log tibble records every automated correction: $flags tibble records everything requiring review:
code: # Full formatted report dq_report(result)  # Access components directly cleaned <- result$data    # tibble with corrections applied + derived columns log     <- result$log     # what was changed automatically flags   <- result$flags   # what needs analyst attention

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: baseline checks can chain additional anomaly detectors. three accept either plain tibble dq_result, return type — compose |>.

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Two columns added $data: pct_change_n_cases anomaly_pct_change_n_cases. Flagged rows also appended $flags.
code: # Aggregate first, then check for unusual week-to-week swings weekly <- dplyr::count(result$data, year, epiweek, province, name = "n_cases")  result <- result |>   add_anomaly_pct_change(     value_col  = "n_cases",     period_col = "epiweek",     year_col   = "year",     group_cols = "province",     threshold  = 0.5   # flag > 50% change   )

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Returns tibble missing epiweek/group combinations, appends $flags given dq_result.
code: gaps <- result |>   add_anomaly_gaps(     period_col  = "epiweek",     period_type = "week",     group_cols  = "province",     year_col    = "year"   )

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Evaluates every rule schema’s consistency: block cleaned data (including derived columns). Use run_dq_checks() derived columns available.

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: Pass list functions run_dq_checks() checks expressed YAML:
code: check_no_future_dates <- function(data, log, flags) {   future_rows <- which(data$sample_date > Sys.Date())   if (length(future_rows) > 0) {     flags <- dplyr::bind_rows(       flags,       tibble::tibble(         row    = future_rows,         column = "sample_date",         value  = as.character(data$sample_date[future_rows]),         issue  = "sample_date is in the future"       )     )   }   list(data = data, log = log, flags = flags) }  result <- run_dq_checks(raw_data, schema, custom_checks = list(check_no_future_dates))

title: Data Quality Pipeline
text: satisfied cleaned data, export downstream use:
code: # Parquet for analysis pipelines arrow::write_parquet(result$data, "data/clean/dr_malaria_clean.parquet")  # Excel summary for review meetings eri_report_excel(   sheets = list(     data  = list(data = result$data,  title = "Cleaned data"),     flags = list(data = result$flags, title = "Flags for review"),     log   = list(data = result$log,   title = "Automated corrections")   ),   path  = "outputs/dq_review.xlsx" )

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Rows population <= 0 NA values automatically return NA avoid silent division errors.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)  weekly <- result$data |>   group_by(year, epiweek, province) |>   summarise(n_cases = n(), .groups = "drop") |>   left_join(population_data, by = c("year", "province")) |>   mutate(rate_per_1000 = eri_incidence_rate(n_cases, population, multiplier = 1000))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Convert CDC epiweek numbers calendar dates time-series plots:
code: weekly <- weekly |>   mutate(week_start = eri_epiweek_date(year, epiweek, week_start = "Sunday"))  library(ggplot2) ggplot(weekly, aes(week_start, n_cases, colour = province)) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Cases", title = "Weekly malaria cases by province") +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme()

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: eri_study_week() expresses epiweeks relative index event — useful intervention analyses IRS campaigns:
code: irs_date <- as.Date("2023-06-15")  weekly <- weekly |>   mutate(study_week = eri_study_week(year, epiweek, index_date = irs_date))  # week < 0 = before IRS; week >= 1 = after IRS pre_post <- weekly |>   mutate(period = ifelse(study_week < 0, "Pre-IRS", "Post-IRS"))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Convert individual case dates epiweek numbers aggregation:
code: # Add epiweek number from a sample date column cases <- cases |>   mutate(epiweek = eri_date_to_epiweek(sample_date))  # Filter to a cross-year epiweek range (week 40/2023 through week 10/2024) subset <- eri_epiweek_range(weekly, "year", "epiweek",                              start_year = 2023, start_week = 40,                              end_year   = 2024, end_week   = 10)

what: Branded ggplot theme and colour palette
title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: ERI figures use eri_brand_ggplot_theme() colours eri_brand_colors():
code: cols <- eri_brand_colors() # navy="#44546A"  blue="#4472C4"  orange="#ED7D31" # gold="#FFC000"  green="#70AD47" light_blue="#5B9BD5"  gray="#A5A5A5"  ggplot(weekly, aes(week_start, rate_per_1000)) +   geom_col(fill = cols[["blue"]]) +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme() +   labs(title = "Malaria incidence rate", y = "Cases per 1 000")

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: LF TAS data typically collected pools (FTS cards). Use eri_lf_pooled_prev() estimate individual-level prevalence pool results using standard formula 1 - ((1 - npos/npool)^(1/pool_size)). function warns (rather errors) npos > npool returns NA rows, batch processing interrupted.
code: # Scalar estimate for a single survey eri_lf_pooled_prev(npos = 3, npool = 100, pool_size = 5)  # Per-commune estimates commune_prev <- tas_data |>   group_by(commune) |>   summarise(     npos      = sum(fts_result == "Positive"),     npool     = n(),     pool_size = mean(pool_size, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups   = "drop"   ) |>   mutate(pooled_prev = eri_lf_pooled_prev(npos, npool, pool_size))

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Use ht_lf_programmatic_treatment dr_lf_programmatic_treatment schemas clean coverage data, compute programme indicators:
code: schema <- load_dq_schema("ht", "lf", "programmatic", "treatment", azcontainer = NULL) result <- run_dq_checks(raw_mda, schema)  coverage <- result$data |>   mutate(     coverage_pct = treated / target_pop * 100,     reached_80   = coverage_pct >= 80   )  dplyr::count(coverage, year, reached_80)

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: Onchocerciasis uses standardised five-level programme status framework. eri_oncho_program_levels() returns ordered factor levels used across OEPA visualisations, eri_oncho_status_map() produces ready--insert ggplot object:
code: eri_oncho_program_levels() # [1] "Non-endemic" # [2] "Under surveillance" # [3] "MDA ongoing" # [4] "MDA stopped - under surveillance" # [5] "Verified free of transmission"  eus <- eri_spatial_load("oepa", level = 2)  status_map <- eri_oncho_status_map(   shapefile   = eus,   status_data = programme_status_2024,   eu_col      = "eu_name",   status_col  = "programme_status",   title       = "OEPA Onchocerciasis Programme Status 2024",   scale_bar   = TRUE,   north_arrow = TRUE )

title: Epidemiological Analytics
text: eri_table() produces Carter Center-branded flextable renders consistently PowerPoint, HTML, Excel:
code: summary_tbl <- weekly |>   group_by(province) |>   summarise(     total_cases = sum(n_cases),     mean_weekly = round(mean(n_cases), 1),     peak_rate   = max(rate_per_1000, na.rm = TRUE)   )  ft <- eri_table(   summary_tbl,   title    = "Malaria summary by province",   footnote = "Source: DIGEPI surveillance data" )  # Insert into a PowerPoint presentation eri_pptx_create() |>   eri_pptx_add_title("Hispaniola Malaria 2024") |>   eri_pptx_add_table(summary_tbl, title = "Province summary") |>   eri_pptx_save("outputs/malaria_2024.pptx")

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions"). Everything offline.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: use bundled case-level malaria schema (Azure needed — azcontainer = NULL loads copy ships package) small synthetic line-list: two provinces six epiweeks, one row per case. three planted problems — species typo, case spike, missing week — kinds thing real extract hides:
code: # Load by the four-part identity (ADR-0012): country, disease, data_source (the # channel — surveillance/programmatic/research), data_type (the measure — here # "case", the case-level line-list). The legacy compound key still works too: # load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria_case") resolves via an alias during the migration. schema <- load_dq_schema("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "case", azcontainer = NULL)  # A tiny helper to make `n` identical case rows for a province/week. mk <- function(province, week, n, species = "P. vivax") {   tibble::tibble(year = 2024, epiweek = week, province = province,                  municipality = paste0(province, " City"),                  sample_date = as.character(as.Date("2024-01-01") + (week - 1) * 7),                  species = species)[rep(1, n), ] }  cases <- dplyr::bind_rows(   mk("Azua", 1, 3), mk("Azua", 1, 1, species = "P.vivax"),   # 1 species typo   mk("Azua", 2, 3), mk("Azua", 3, 4), mk("Azua", 4, 3),   mk("Azua", 5, 15), mk("Azua", 6, 4),                        # week 5 SPIKE   mk("San Juan", 1, 3), mk("San Juan", 2, 3), mk("San Juan", 3, 3),   mk("San Juan", 5, 3), mk("San Juan", 6, 3)                  # week 4 MISSING ) nrow(cases)   # 48 cases across 2 provinces, epiweeks 1-6

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: Start run_dq_checks() — engine ingest guide covers. catches cell problems (bad types, --range values, values allowed list): One flag — mistyped species. Useful, tells nothing whether epidemiology makes sense. , chain anomaly detectors.
code: result <- run_dq_checks(cases, schema) result #> ✔ DQ checks complete: 0 corrections, 1 flag for review. #> ── Data Quality Report ────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Shape: 48 rows x 7 columns #> #> ── Automated Corrections (0 total) ── #> ✔ No corrections applied. #> #> ── Flags Requiring Review (1 total) ── #> ! Value not in allowed_values list: 1 row [species] (e.g. P.vivax (row 4)) #> #> ℹ See `result$flags` for the full row-level detail.

title: Catching anomalies in a new surveillance extract (for epidemiologists)
text: Epidemic data moves week week; sudden jump either real outbreak data error, need know . Aggregate weekly counts per province, flag period--period changes threshold: Azua jumped 3 cases 15 week 5 — five-fold rise (detector reports pct_change = 4, .e. +400%) — fell back week 6 (-73%). flagged, spike produces two large changes (, ). week-5 row one investigate: genuine cluster field respond , batch cases get entered twice? detector doesn’t decide — points week worth phone call. (aggregated result$data -, one still-flagged species row rolled — harmless per-week counts, real extract ’d resolve cell flags §2 rolling anything .)
code: weekly <- count(result$data, year, epiweek, province, name = "n_cases")  flagged <- add_anomaly_pct_change(   weekly,   value_col  = "n_cases",   period_col = "epiweek",   group_cols = "province",   # compare each province against itself   year_col   = "year",       # so weeks order correctly across years   threshold  = 0.5           # flag changes greater than 50% ) #> ! 2 rows flagged for % change anomaly in "n_cases" (threshold: 50%).  flagged[flagged$anomaly_pct_change_n_cases, c("epiweek", "province", "n_cases", "pct_change_n_cases")] #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   epiweek province n_cases pct_change_n_cases #>     <dbl> <chr>      <int>              <dbl> #> 1       5 Azua          15              4 #> 2       6 Azua           4             -0.733

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions"), plus sf package (install.packages("sf")). string-match step runs fully offline. optional geocoding step uses OpenStreetMap (Nominatim) default — keyless, setup — though makes network calls. what: 1. The reference boundary and your messy data
title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: Reconciliation needs authoritative boundary — sf object whose columns hold canonical admin names. real work load eri_spatial_load(); guide build tiny one hand (two real places live geocoder somewhere land): incoming data — five localities usual problems (case, typo, names boundary, one pure noise): describe hierarchy two parallel vectors, finest → coarsest: data’s columns (loc_cols) matching boundary columns (admin_cols).
code: box <- function(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) {   st_polygon(list(matrix(c(xmin,ymin, xmax,ymin, xmax,ymax, xmin,ymax, xmin,ymin),                          ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE))) } boundary <- st_sf(   adm3_name = c("Boston", "Cambridge"),   adm2_name = c("Suffolk", "Middlesex"),     # the authoritative county for each   adm1_name = c("Massachusetts", "Massachusetts"),   geometry  = st_sfc(box(-71.10, -70.99, 42.230, 42.350),                      box(-71.16, -71.06, 42.350, 42.400), crs = 4326) ) messy <- tibble::tibble(   locality = c("boston", "Cambrige", "Fenway Park", "MIT", "Zzqxborough Nowhere"),   county   = c("Suffolk", "Middlesex", "Suffolk",   "Suffolk", "Suffolk"),   state    = rep("Massachusetts", 5),   cases    = c(12L, 7L, 4L, 3L, 1L) ) messy #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   locality            county    state         cases #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>         <int> #> 1 boston              Suffolk   Massachusetts    12 #> 2 Cambrige            Middlesex Massachusetts     7 #> 3 Fenway Park         Suffolk   Massachusetts     4 #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk   Massachusetts     3 #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk   Massachusetts     1

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: First, match can network . Setting method = NULL disables geocoding entirely, max_dist = 1 allows one-character typo finest level: Two wins already, offline: "boston" matched Boston (case-insensitive, accent-insensitive), typo "Cambrige" matched Cambridge (within max_dist). matched row whole hierarchy rewritten boundary’s canonical spelling — just locality, county state — misspelled parent gets fixed well. three don’t appear boundary, ’re unresolved — ’re residual geocode.
code: matched <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   messy,   loc_cols   = c("locality", "county", "state"),   admin_cols = c("adm3_name", "adm2_name", "adm1_name"),   shapefile  = boundary,   method     = NULL,    # string match only — fully offline   max_dist   = 1L       # tolerate a 1-character typo in the finest name ) #> ✔ Reconciled 5 distinct localities: 2 matched, 0 geocoded, 0 need review, 3 unresolved.  matched[, c("locality", "county", "state", "reconcile_status")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   locality            county    state         reconcile_status #>   <chr>               <chr>     <chr>         <chr> #> 1 Boston              Suffolk   Massachusetts matched #> 2 Cambridge           Middlesex Massachusetts matched #> 3 Fenway Park         Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk   Massachusetts unresolved

title: Reconciling free-text localities to admin units (for epidemiologists)
text: Now let geocoding handle rows didn’t match. method = "osm" uses keyless OpenStreetMap; country_name appended address anchor : Three different outcomes three residual rows: Fenway Park → geocoded. geocoded point inside Boston/Suffolk polygon, agrees county supplied — ’s trusted: name rewritten canonical Boston get coordinates. MIT → geocoded_review. geocoded fine, point falls Cambridge/Middlesex polygon — claimed Suffolk. disagreement trust guard firing: coordinates kept inspect, names left exactly supplied (silently “corrected”). Maybe MIT really across county line expected — maybe county mis-entered. decide. Zzqxborough Nowhere → unresolved. geocoder returned nothing. coordinates, nothing changed. don’t take “Cambridge/Middlesex” faith. eri_spatial_reconcile() returns admin units geocoded point actually fell geocoded_<admin_col> columns — flagged row tells exactly disagreed , manual sf::st_join() re-derive : county still Suffolk (untouched — trust guard never overwrote ), geocoded_adm2_name shows point landed Middlesex. side--side whole conflict, ready confirm fix. (columns NA matched, unresolved, outside-polygon rows.)
code: result <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   messy,   loc_cols     = c("locality", "county", "state"),   admin_cols   = c("adm3_name", "adm2_name", "adm1_name"),   shapefile    = boundary,   country_name = "USA",   method       = "osm",   max_dist     = 1L ) #> ℹ Geocoding 3 unmatched localities via "osm"... #> ✔ Reconciled 5 distinct localities: 2 matched, 1 geocoded, 1 need review, 1 unresolved. #> ! 1 geocode flagged "geocoded_review" (low-confidence or parent mismatch) -- verify before use.  result[, c("locality", "county", "longitude", "latitude", "reconcile_status")] #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   locality            county    longitude latitude reconcile_status #>   <chr>               <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl> <chr> #> 1 Boston              Suffolk         NA      NA   matched #> 2 Cambridge           Middlesex       NA      NA   matched #> 3 Boston              Suffolk      -71.1     42.3  geocoded #> 4 MIT                 Suffolk      -71.1     42.4  geocoded_review #> 5 Zzqxborough Nowhere Suffolk         NA      NA   unresolved result[result$reconcile_status == "geocoded_review",        c("locality", "county", "geocoded_adm3_name", "geocoded_adm2_name")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   locality county  geocoded_adm3_name geocoded_adm2_name #>   <chr>    <chr>   <chr>              <chr> #> 1 MIT      Suffolk Cambridge          Middlesex
