Sourcing canonical, team-approved data. study draws data analysts already approved (study artifact uploaded), discover catalog pull coordinates — eri_research_pull() takes four tokens eri_catalog_query() reports (country, disease, data_source, data_type): Discover exists, pull coordinates catalog handed — one vocabulary, end end. Never commit data/ git. scaffold’s .gitignore already excludes . Data lives Azure; repository holds code research.yaml record pulled. mtcars matter, habit keeps real, protected country data safe — see Clean .
code: eri_research_pull(path = "artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv", dest = "data") #> ✔ Pulled 1 file from artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv to data. eri_catalog_query(country = "dr", disease = "malaria", layer = "processed") #> # ... data_source = surveillance, data_type = case ... eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria",                   data_source = "surveillance", data_type = "case", dest = "data")

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: analysis goes analysis/workflow.qmd project — scaffold seeded , gets version-controlled reproducibility-checked. Open write ordinary R. Read data just pulled, fit model: whole analysis. Notice none used erifunctions — modelling . package’s job starts want save freeze found.
code: cars <- read.csv("data/cars.csv", row.names = 1)  model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = cars) summary(model) #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = cars) #>  #> Residuals: #>     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max  #> -2.9515 -1.4818 -0.1307  1.4072  3.3112  #>  #> Coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept) 30.6052577  2.6986421  11.341 9.32e-09 *** #> wt          -1.8050509  1.0698184  -1.687   0.1122     #> hp          -0.0401358  0.0160570  -2.500   0.0245 *   #> disp        -0.0007664  0.0100435  -0.076   0.9402     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 2.024 on 15 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.7677, Adjusted R-squared:  0.7212  #> F-statistic: 16.52 on 3 and 15 DF,  p-value: 5.09e-05

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Push results back Azure safe tied project. Save fitted model object, figure: make decisions, jot project’s logbook. notes timestamped stay project — “drop rows?” gets answered year later:
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(cars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs. weight (automatic cars)",        x = "Weight (1000 lbs)", y = "Miles per gallon") ggsave("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png", p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150) eri_research_upload_output(model, "mtcars_lm.qs2") #> ✔ Output "mtcars_lm.qs2" uploaded to research/mtcars_study/outputs/mtcars_lm.qs2.  eri_research_upload_figure(   "figs/mpg_vs_weight.png",   caption = "MPG vs. weight, automatic cars (initial dataset)." ) #> ✔ Figure "mpg_vs_weight.png" uploaded to research/mtcars_study/outputs/figs/mpg_vs_weight.png. eri_research_log("Started with automatic transmissions only (am == 0); 19 cars.") eri_research_log("Fit lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp). Weight is the dominant predictor.")

what: 7. Look at the state of your project
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: point, ask project stands. eri_research_status() prints data pulled, many times input updated, many outputs, snapshots, tags exist: eri_research_resume() shorter “?” view — last pull, last logbook entry, snapshot count. Use top every work session (§9).
code: eri_research_status() #> ── Research project: mtcars_study (us/demo) ── #> • 1 tracked input (1 pull, 0 artifacts) #> • 2 outputs, 0 snapshots, 0 tags, 0 promotions #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind  source                                          pulled_at            updates archived #>   <chr> <chr>                                           <chr>                  <int> <lgl> #> 1 pull  artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv 2026-06-17T15:01:00Z       0 FALSE

what: 8. Save a figure again later (updating it)
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Figures evolve. improve one, just re-upload filename — replaces previous version Azure project keeps pointing current figure. special “update” command; uploading name update. (exactly §11 data changes.)

what: 9. Pause work — and pick it up weeks later
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Real studies stop start. put one safely find way back.

previous_headings: 9. Pause work — and pick it up weeks later
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: walk away, take snapshot — timestamped freeze whole data/ directory Azure, exact inputs recoverable even file changes later. Leave note, commit code, done: commit code (usethis::use_git() first commit, Commit main button GitHub Desktop) close laptop. Nothing lost.
code: eri_research_snapshot(label = "before-break") #> ✔ Snapshot of 1 file saved to research/mtcars_study/snapshots/20260617T150500Z/.  eri_research_log("Pausing here. Initial model done; awaiting the fuller dataset.")

previous_headings: 9. Pause work — and pick it up weeks later
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Reopen project RStudio (open project, loose files, renv re-activates pinned environment automatically). ask project reintroduce : resume() (?) status() (?), oriented minute, however long .
code: library(erifunctions) eri_research_resume() #> ✔ Project: 'mtcars_study' (us / demo) #>   Azure:      research/mtcars_study/ #>   Last pull:  2026-06-17T15:00:00Z #>   Last log:   [2026-06-17T15:05:00Z] Pausing here. Initial model done; awaiting the fuller dataset. #>   Snapshots:  1  eri_research_status()   # the fuller picture: inputs, outputs, tags

what: 10. When — and how — to tag a version
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: tag freezes whole study moment — data snapshot, exact analysis code commit, outputs — name can cite. answer “reproduce numbers report dated March.” tag? milestones, schedule. Good moments: finished analysis share present; make big change; submitting publishing. tag every day — tag marks significant progress, tags immutable, flurry throwaway tags just adds noise. (Snapshots, §9, cheap, frequent safety net; tags rare, citable landmarks.) just finished first real result, tag . Make sure analysis code committed first (tag records commit, warns working tree uncommitted changes): tag now binds data + code + outputs together forever. Anyone label can reconstruct exactly .
code: eri_research_tag(   "mtcars-am0-v1",   description = "Initial model: mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, automatic transmissions only." ) #> ✔ Tagged "mtcars-am0-v1": snapshot 20260617T150500Z + code "a1b2c3d".

what: 11. New data arrives — update and re-run
title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: Months , fuller dataset lands: longer restricted automatic cars. moment workflows handle badly — overwriting old data losing trail. erifunctions handles cleanly: old version archived, change counted, earlier tag still reproduces original. Build expanded dataset (drop == 0 filter — cars now) re-upload artifact name, bumping version. Re-using name update, duplicate: Now pull new version project. pulled input , erifunctions archives previous copy, swaps new one, records input now updated: See updates ticked 1 archived now TRUE — original 19-car extract safely archived, gone. Re-run analysis fuller data, update figure (filename — §8), log change, tag new version: now two citable versions: mtcars-am0-v1 (original) mtcars-full-v1 (update). first still reproduces original analysis exactly, even though data moved — whole point.
code: cars_v2 <- mtcars          # the full dataset now — both transmission types nrow(cars_v2)              # 32 cars, up from 19 #> [1] 32 write.csv(cars_v2, "data/cars.csv", row.names = TRUE)  eri_artifact_upload(   local_path  = "data/cars.csv",   name        = "mtcars_study_data",      # same name → updates the existing artifact   type        = "study_data",   description = "mtcars fuel-economy study data — all transmissions (expanded).",   version     = "2.0" ) eri_research_pull(path = "artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv", dest = "data") #> ℹ Archived 1 prior version to data/_archive/20260701T090000Z. #> ✔ Pulled 1 file from artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv to data.  eri_research_status() #> ── Research project: mtcars_study (us/demo) ── #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind  source                                          pulled_at            updates archived #>   <chr> <chr>                                           <chr>                  <int> <lgl> #> 1 pull  artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data/cars.csv 2026-07-01T09:00:00Z       1 TRUE cars <- mtcars model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = cars) coef(model) #>   (Intercept)            wt            hp          disp  #> 37.1055052690 -3.8008905826 -0.0311565508 -0.0009370091 library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(cars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Fuel economy vs. weight (all cars)") ggsave("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png", p, width = 6, height = 4, dpi = 150)  # same name = update  eri_research_upload_output(model, "mtcars_lm.qs2") eri_research_upload_figure("figs/mpg_vs_weight.png",                            caption = "MPG vs. weight, all cars (expanded dataset).") eri_research_log("Expanded to the full dataset (32 cars). Re-fit and updated the figure.")  # Snapshot the new data first, so the tag binds *this* (full) dataset rather than the # earlier "before-break" snapshot. eri_research_snapshot(label = "full-data") eri_research_tag(   "mtcars-full-v1",   description = "Re-run on the full dataset (all transmissions)." )

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: ran walkthrough practice, remove everything created leave trace Azure. deletes project folder, snapshots tags, artifact: Confirm nothing remains, delete local folder: delete freely : mtcars public sample data. Real Carter Center data different — country surveillance study data must never deleted casually, copied Azure, committed git. discipline guide builds (data stays Azure, pulled provenance, frozen tags) exactly keeps protected data safe. doubt, ask delete.
code: con <- get_azure_storage_connection(storage_name = "data")  # eri_dir_delete() removes a non-empty directory recursively in one call — the # in-package path, so cleanup stays in erifunctions instead of raw AzureStor. eri_dir_delete("research/mtcars_study", azcontainer = con) eri_dir_delete("artifacts/study_data/mtcars_study_data", azcontainer = con) eri_research_list()       # mtcars_study should be gone eri_artifact_list()       # mtcars_study_data should be gone  unlink("../mtcars_study", recursive = TRUE)   # remove the local project folder

title: A complete research workflow for epidemiologists
text: now done full research lifecycle: start, version-control, add source data, analyse, save, tag, pause, resume, handle new data, tidy . first planned set short, task-oriented guides — one common job analyst epidemiologist (running monthly country report, ingesting surveillance dataset, onboarding new country, ). See guide index exists today coming. full menu functions grouped purpose, see reference.

title: Getting started with erifunctions
text: Azure access zero-config — first command needs opens browser sign . ODK, SharePoint, Teams need little setup. Connecting Azure, ODK, SharePoint & Teams guide walks confirm works — including one thing first governed action: set ERI_ANALYST_ID approvals logs carry name, OS username.
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions") library(erifunctions)

what: 2. Learn the one idea: how data is addressed
title: Getting started with erifunctions
text: Every dataset lives data/ Azure blob five-axis path (ADR-0012): idea makes whole system click: data arrives (data_source) separate measures (data_type). surveillance channel case line-list one country aggregate weekly count another; one CMR fans treatment, mmdp, survey measures across diseases. Run eri_data_model() — prints whole vocabulary, never guess value: Everything moves three layers — raw/ (received) → staged/ (DQ-checked) → processed/ (canonical data whole team trusts). Nothing reaches processed/ without human calling eri_approve().
code: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ eri_data_model() #> ── Data-addressing model (ADR-0012) ── #> Path: 'data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/' #> #> ── data_source (channel / how the data arrives) ── #> • surveillance -- Direct disease-output feed (e.g. a MoH surveillance system). #> • programmatic -- Programmatic activity/coverage data (CMR, MDA feeds); spans diseases. #> • research     -- Research surveys/studies; DA-managed, flexible measure. #>   (… plus the measures, formats, and layers, and the transitional cmr/odk tokens.)

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: eri_sharepoint_connect() opens browser window interactive authentication first time. token cached AzureAuth subsequent calls within session re-prompt.
code: library(erifunctions)  site <- eri_sharepoint_connect("https://cartercenter.sharepoint.com/sites/ERI")

what: 2. List files in a document library folder
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: Filter find recent weekly upload:
code: # List the root of the default document library files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site)  # List a specific subfolder weekly_files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti") print(weekly_files) # A tibble with columns: name, size, modified, is_folder, path library(dplyr)  latest <- weekly_files |>   filter(!is_folder, grepl("\\.xlsx$", name)) |>   arrange(desc(modified)) |>   slice(1)  cat("Latest file:", latest$name, "\n") cat("Modified:   ", format(latest$modified), "\n")

what: 3. Read a file directly into R
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: eri_sharepoint_read() downloads file temp location reads based extension (.xlsx, .csv, .parquet, .rds). unknown extension function returns local temp path can read manually:
code: # Read an Excel file df <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti/ht_weekly_2024.xlsx") head(df) # Read a CSV file with extra readr arguments df_csv <- eri_sharepoint_read(   site,   "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/DR/dr_cases_2024.csv",   col_types = readr::cols(.default = "c") ) # Read a specific Excel sheet df_sheet <- eri_sharepoint_read(   site,   "Shared Documents/Reports/annual_summary.xlsx",   sheet = "Malaria" ) path <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, "Shared Documents/Data/custom_format.sav") # path is a character string -- use haven::read_sav(path) or similar

what: 4. Upload a finished report to SharePoint
title: SharePoint Workflow
text: destination folder created automatically exist. Use overwrite = FALSE prevent accidentally replacing file: function returns SharePoint item URL invisibly:
code: # Push a local Excel report to the team SharePoint eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = "outputs/ht_malaria_weekly_summary.xlsx",   site        = site,   folder_path = "Shared Documents/Reports/Haiti/2024" ) eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = "outputs/ht_malaria_weekly_summary.xlsx",   site        = site,   folder_path = "Shared Documents/Reports/Haiti/2024",   overwrite   = FALSE   # errors if the file already exists ) url <- eri_sharepoint_upload("outputs/report.xlsx", site, "Shared Documents/Reports") cat("Shared at:", url, "\n")

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: common pattern : pull data SharePoint, run DQ checks, generate report, push report back.
code: library(erifunctions) library(dplyr)  # ---- Step 1: connect ---- site <- eri_sharepoint_connect("https://cartercenter.sharepoint.com/sites/ERI")  # ---- Step 2: pull the latest weekly submission ---- files <- eri_sharepoint_list(site, "Shared Documents/Weekly Data/Haiti") latest_path <- files |>   filter(!is_folder, grepl("\\.xlsx$", name)) |>   arrange(desc(modified)) |>   slice(1) |>   pull(path)  raw <- eri_sharepoint_read(site, latest_path)  # ---- Step 3: run DQ checks ---- schema <- load_dq_schema("haiti", "malaria", azcontainer = NULL) result <- run_dq_checks(raw, schema)  # ---- Step 4: generate a summary Excel report ---- tmp_report <- tempfile(fileext = ".xlsx") eri_report_excel(   list(     data   = list(data = result$data),     flags  = list(data = result$flags)   ),   path = tmp_report )  # ---- Step 5: push back to SharePoint ---- week_label <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-W%V") eri_sharepoint_upload(   local_path  = tmp_report,   site        = site,   folder_path = paste0("Shared Documents/DQ Reports/Haiti/", week_label) )

title: SharePoint Workflow
text: Tokens cached ~/.AzureR/ AzureAuth. prompted every session, check ~/.AzureR/ writable. scripted / CI use, prefer service-principal auth configured Microsoft365R::create_sharepoint_site() auth_type = "client_credentials". eri_sharepoint_* functions guard Microsoft365R installed give clear install instruction absent.

title: Spatial Workflow
text: Admin boundaries stored Azure spatial/{country}/adm{level}.rds validated sf objects. Levels follow standard hierarchy: 0 = country, 1 = department/region, 2 = province/commune, 3 = municipality/locality, 4 = sub-locality. Every object loaded Azure validated : - defined CRS (WGS 84, EPSG 4326) - empty geometries - canonical name column adm{level}_name
code: library(erifunctions)  # Load Haiti commune (adm2) boundaries communes <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Load DR province (adm2) boundaries provinces <- eri_spatial_load("dr", level = 2)

title: Spatial Workflow
text: adding new country updating boundaries, use eri_spatial_upload(). validates shapefile accepting upload: function blocks descriptive error validation check fails, explaining exactly fix.
code: # Prepare your shapefile first: #   - Set CRS to 4326 if not already set #   - Rename the admin name column to adm2_name #   - Remove any empty geometries  eri_spatial_upload(   local_path = "data/shapefiles/hti_adm2.shp",   country    = "ht",   level      = 2 )

what: Spatial join: GPS coordinates to admin units
title: Spatial Workflow
text: Use eri_spatial_join() assign admin unit names point data lat/lon columns. Rows missing coordinates dropped warning. result plain tibble — geometry dropped automatically, downstream analysis need handle sf objects.
code: # Load the reference boundary communes <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Attach commune and department names to case records case_data <- eri_spatial_join(   data       = raw_cases,   lat_col    = "latitude",   lon_col    = "longitude",   shapefile  = communes,   admin_cols = c("adm2_name", "adm1_name") )

title: Spatial Workflow
text: composing maps often useful add buffer around study area boundary features fully visible. eri_bbox_expand() takes sf bounding box expands specified distance metres:
code: library(sf)  haiti <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 0) bbox  <- sf::st_bbox(haiti) |>   eri_bbox_expand(X = 30000, Y = 30000)   # 30 km padding on all sides  # Use the expanded bbox as a crop/map extent

title: Spatial Workflow
text: Combine eri_spatial_load() eri_brand_ggplot_theme() standard ggplot2 / sf functions produce branded choropleth maps.
code: library(ggplot2) library(sf)  # Aggregate case counts by commune case_counts <- dplyr::count(case_data, adm2_name, name = "n_cases")  # Join to boundaries map_data <- dplyr::left_join(communes, case_counts, by = "adm2_name")  ggplot(map_data) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = n_cases), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_gradient(     low  = "#E8F4FD",     high = eri_brand_colors()[["navy"]],     na.value = "grey90",     name = "Cases"   ) +   labs(title = "Malaria cases by commune") +   eri_brand_ggplot_theme()

title: Spatial Workflow
text: LandScan global population rasters stored Azure spatial/landscan/. Use eri_spatial_load_landscan() (R/spatial.R) load raster given year, extract population admin unit using exactextractr package:
code: library(terra) library(exactextractr)  pop_raster <- eri_spatial_load_landscan(year = 2023) communes   <- eri_spatial_load("ht", level = 2)  # Sum population pixels within each commune communes$population <- exact_extract(pop_raster, communes, "sum")

title: Spatial Workflow
text: OEPA onchocerciasis data, eri_oncho_status_map() wraps full mapping pipeline — joins status data shapefile, applies standard ERI programme-status colour palette, adds scale bar north arrow:
code: eus     <- eri_spatial_load("oepa", level = 2)  # evaluation unit boundaries status  <- read_dq_result("outputs/oepa_status_2024.parquet")  map <- eri_oncho_status_map(   shapefile   = eus,   status_data = status,   eu_col      = "eu_name",   status_col  = "programme_status",   title       = "OEPA Onchocerciasis Programme Status 2024" )  eri_pptx_create() |>   eri_pptx_add_plot(map, title = "Programme Status") |>   eri_pptx_save("outputs/oepa_status_report.pptx")

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: criteria automated reviewer (.github/workflows/claude-review.yaml) uses. Edit file tune review — version-controlled purpose, way ADRs , review standard evolves project.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: : advisory, independent check change fits project’s design conventions. reviewer write code read canon fresh time. Isn’t: correctness/test gate. R-CMD-check pkgdown CI pass/fail; re-litigate . Never approve merge — human makes final call. Raise questions directly review something ambiguous.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: CLAUDE.md — conventions + “global vs local solution” guardrail. docs/roadmap.md — V2 phases (isn’t) meant deliver. docs/adr/ — accepted architecture decisions.

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: Reuse re-implementation. change re-implement something already exists (azure_io(), eri_read()/eri_write(), eri_data_path(), DQ/catalog/ research machinery)? Flag duplication; point existing helper. Global vs local solution. problem general (countries/diseases/users hit ), solved generally — one-buried single workflow? ADR-0002: metadata-store writes must concurrency-safe (ETag/optimistic), naive read-modify-write. ADR-0003: approver/actor identity comes verified token, self-declared env var. ADR-0005: pull--process; provenance recorded pull entry points — analytic helpers shouldn’t fetch implicitly. ADR-0006: research/analysis code lives separate repos, package source. change intentionally revisits decision, add/supersede ADR PR — flag doesn’t. Conventions (CLAUDE.md): exported eri_* / internal .eri_*; cli::cli_* user-facing messages; roxygen @examples (live calls \dontrun{}); one domain per file R/; NAMESPACE/man/ regenerated exports/roxygen change. User-first. Users Data Analysts Epidemiologists, developers — public surface clear, documented, hard misuse? Docs sync. change alters plan decision, docs/roadmap.md / ADRs updated PR?

title: Claude PR review guidelines
text: Post one PR review. Use inline comments can point specific line. Request changes clear violations; otherwise comment. Keep concise cite specific doc/section (e.g. “ADR-0006”) maintainer can act quickly.

title: ERI team functions

title: ERI team functions
text: copy-paste, start--finish walkthroughs fastest way learn system. Read documentation site, groups Get started → role → topic deep-dives → contributing. Getting started article front door. New ? order (dip rest work needs ). First, run eri_data_model() — prints data-addressing vocabulary (channel vs. measure) every guide assumes. New Data Analyst: Connections → Onboard space → Ingest dataset → Triage log backlog. CMR ODK file . New Epidemiologist: Connections → Research workflow → Reconcile localities → Catch anomalies. full set: full, grouped function reference lives https://thecartercenter.github.io/erifunctions/reference/.

title: ERI team functions
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("thecartercenter/erifunctions")  # Pin the version in your analysis project (recommended) renv::install("thecartercenter/erifunctions") renv::snapshot()

title: ERI team functions
text: Add following project .Renviron (usethis::edit_r_environ(scope = "project")): Restart R editing .Renviron.
code: # Azure storage ERIFUNCTIONS_TENANT_ID=<Azure tenant ID> ERIFUNCTIONS_APP_ID=<Azure app registration ID> ERIFUNCTIONS_RESOURCE_ENDPOINT=<storage account endpoint URL> ERIFUNCTIONS_STORAGE_NAME=projects ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME=data  # Service principal — for scripted/automated use only ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID=<SP client ID> ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET=<SP client secret>  # Your analyst identity (appears in approval and access logs) ERI_ANALYST_ID=firstname.lastname  # ODK Central ODK_URL=https://rblf.tccodk.org/ ODK_USER=your.email@cartercenter.org ODK_PASS=<ODK password>

title: ERI team functions
text: Two templates ship package. Pull either one eri_template_pull(): Open .qmd file RStudio knit .
code: # Data analyst daily workflow (connections, ODK sync, DQ, approve) eri_template_pull("eri_daily_workflow")  # Epidemiologist research workflow (init/resume, pull data, analysis, snapshot) eri_template_pull("eri_research_workflow")

title: ERI team functions
text: data lives data/ Azure blob standard path (ADR-0012): data_type (measure) level optional — channel-data lands one level shallower. two addressing axes explained just . eri_approve() explicit human gate. Nothing reaches processed/ without .
code: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/                                                    raw/        <- as-received from source                                                    staged/     <- DQ-checked, awaiting approval                                                    processed/  <- analyst-approved, canonical

title: ERI team functions
text: path five independent axes (ADR-0012) — key data arrives (data_source) separate measures (data_type): One source can carry many measures (CMR yields treatment + mmdp + survey; source + disease case one country, aggregate another). data_source data_type extensible — eri_data_model() lists known values, unregistered one warns rather errors new data never blocked. (schema set keyed country / disease / data_source / data_type; legacy four-axis path form schema names migrated #175.)

what: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: Evaluates named consistency rules schema's consistency block flags rows rule violated. rule specifies lhs column, comparison op, either rhs column rhs_value constant. Schema format (add consistency: block YAML schema):   Supported operators: <=, >=, ==, <, >, !=. Missing values (NA) either operand skip check row. Works plain tibble (returns tibble violations) dq_result (appends violations $flags).
code: consistency:   positives_le_tested:     lhs: NumMicroPos     op: "<="     rhs: NumTestedMicro     message: "Positive cases exceed tested"   age_non_negative:     lhs: Age     op: ">="     rhs_value: 0     message: "Age is negative"

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
code: add_anomaly_consistency(data, schema)

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: data tibble dq_result object. schema Named list load_dq_schema().

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
text: tibble violations columns row, column, value, issue (includes rule name message). input dq_result, violations appended $flags updated dq_result returned. Returns empty tibble rules pass.

title: Flag cross-field consistency violations defined in a schema — add_anomaly_consistency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ schema <- load_dq_schema("haiti", "malaria") run_dq_checks(data, schema) |> add_anomaly_consistency(schema) } # }

what: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: Identifies gaps time series inferring full expected sequence periods observed minimum maximum, returning rows missing periods. Works plain tibble dq_result object. Supports two period types: "week" — expects contiguous integers 1–53 within year. gap year boundary (week 52/53 → week 1 next year) handled correctly. "month" — expects contiguous integers 1–12 within year.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
code: add_anomaly_gaps(   data,   period_col,   period_type = c("week", "month"),   group_cols = NULL,   year_col = NULL )

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: data tibble dq_result object. period_col str Column containing period value (integer week 1–53 month 1–12). period_type str One "week" "month". group_cols chr vector columns check gaps within group (e.g. c("Province_Residence")). Default NULL checks full dataset. year_col str NULL Column containing year. Required period_type = "week" "month" detect cross-year gaps.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
text: tibble missing periods columns year (year_col supplied), period, group_cols, issue = "structural_gap". input dq_result, missing-period rows also appended $flags (row = NA). Returns empty tibble gaps found.

title: Flag missing time periods in surveillance data — add_anomaly_gaps
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gaps <- add_anomaly_gaps(agg_data, "EpiWeek", "week",                           group_cols = "Province_Residence", year_col = "Year") } # }

what: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: Computes period--period percent change numeric column flags rows whose absolute change exceeds threshold. Works plain tibble dq_result object returned run_dq_checks(), enabling chaining:   passed dq_result, anomaly rows appended $flags percent-change columns added $data.
code: run_dq_checks(data, schema) |>   add_anomaly_pct_change("n_cases", "EpiWeek", group_cols = "Province_Residence")

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
code: add_anomaly_pct_change(   data,   value_col,   period_col,   threshold = 0.5,   group_cols = NULL,   year_col = NULL )

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: data tibble dq_result object. value_col str Name numeric column check. period_col str Name column defines time order within group (e.g. "EpiWeek", "month"). threshold num Absolute percent change threshold (proportion). Default 0.5 flags changes greater 50%. group_cols chr vector column names group computing change (e.g. c("Province_Residence", "disease")). Default NULL treats whole dataset one group. year_col str NULL period_col resets year (e.g. "EpiWeek" 1–53), supply year column ordering correct across year boundaries. Default NULL.

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
text: input object two columns added data: pct_change_{value_col} (numeric) anomaly_pct_change_{value_col} (logical). input dq_result, flagged rows also appended $flags.

title: Flag rows with unusual period-over-period percent change — add_anomaly_pct_change
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ agg <- dplyr::count(raw_dr, Year, EpiWeek, Province_Residence, name = "n_cases") agg_flagged <- add_anomaly_pct_change(agg, "n_cases", "EpiWeek",                                        group_cols = "Province_Residence",                                        year_col   = "Year") } # }

what: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: function serves primary way interact Azure system R. can read, write, create folders, check whether file folder exists, upload files, list files folder.

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
code: azure_io(   io,   file_loc = NULL,   obj = NULL,   azcontainer = suppressMessages(get_azure_storage_connection()),   force_delete = FALSE,   local_path = NULL,   progress = FALSE,   ... )

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: io str type operation perform Azure "read" Read file Azure, must rds, csv, rda, xls/xlsx file. "write" Write file Azure, must rds, csv, rda, xls/xlsx file. write Excel file multiple sheets, pass named list containing tibbles interest. See examples. "exists.dir" Returns boolean checking see folder exists. "exists.file"Returns boolean checking see file exists. "create" Creates folder preceding folders. "list" Returns tibble objects folder. "upload" Moves file type Azure "delete" Deletes file. "delete.dir" Deletes folder. file_loc str Location "read", "write", "exists.dir", "exists.file", "create" "list". obj robj Object saved, needed "write". Defaults NULL. azcontainer Azure container object returned get_azure_storage_connection(). force_delete logical Use delete io without confirmation prompt. Default FALSE. local_path str Local file pathway upload file Azure. Default NULL. parameter required passing "upload" io parameter. progress logical Show AzureStor's byte progress bar transfer. Default FALSE (suppressed). Set TRUE large single read/upload needs visible feedback. ... Optional parameters work readr::read_delim(), readxl::read_excel(), ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
text: Output dependent argument passed io parameter.

title: Helper function to read and write key data to the Azure environment — azure_io
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- azure_io("read", file_loc = "df1.csv") df2 <- azure_io("read", file_loc = "df2.xlsx", sheet = 1, skip = 2) list_of_df <- list(df_1 = df, df_2 = df) azure_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.csv", obj = df) azure_io("write", file_loc = "Data/test/df.xlsx", obj = list_of_df) azure_io("exists.dir", "Data/nonexistentfolder") azure_io("exists.file", file_loc = "Data/test/df1.csv") azure_io("create", "Data/nonexistentfolder") azure_io("list") azure_io("upload", file_loc = "Data/test", local_path = "C:/Users/ABC1/Desktop/df2.csv") } # }

what: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
title: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
text: human approval gate three-layer pipeline. Finds files staged/ directory whose names contain period, moves processed/, writes YAML approval log alongside . Analyst identity read ERI_ANALYST_ID environment variable. unset, approver recorded "<os-username> (unverified)" (one-time warning emitted fallback silent); set ERI_REQUIRE_ANALYST_ID truthy refuse approving without configured identity instead. operation log capturing every step (including errors) always written {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/logs/ data container, regardless whether approval succeeds fails. log primary debugging artifact pipeline issues.

title: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
code: eri_approve(   country,   disease,   data_source,   period,   data_type = NULL,   azcontainer = NULL )

title: Approve staged data and promote it to processed — eri_approve
text: country str Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht"). disease str Disease name (e.g. "malaria"). data_source str channel data came : "surveillance", "programmatic", "research" (ADR-0012). period str Period string matched staged filenames (e.g. "2024-W01", "2024-01"). staged file whose name contains string promoted. data_type str NULL measure data captures (e.g. "case", "aggregate", "treatment", "tas"). NULL (default) approves legacy four-axis path {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/... without measure level, catalog entry's measure recorded NA — one-time per-session note points deliberate choice, silent omission. azcontainer Azure container object data/ blob, returned get_azure_storage_connection(). NULL (default), connects automatically using ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME.

what: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
text: Prints (returns invisibly) registry known values five-axis canonical path data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ (ADR-0012): data_source channels, data_type measures, input formats, pipeline layers. New sources/measures added registry onboarding; unregistered value warns rather errors.

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
code: eri_data_model()

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
text: Invisibly, registry named list.

title: Show the data-addressing model: known sources, measures and formats — eri_data_model
code: eri_data_model() #>  #> ── Data-addressing model (ADR-0012) ──────────────────────────────────────────── #> Path: data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/ #>  #> ── data_source (channel / how the data arrives) ── #>  #> • surveillance -- Direct disease-output feed (e.g. a Ministry-of-Health #>   surveillance system). #> • programmatic -- Programmatic activity/coverage data (country-team CMR, MoH #>   MDA feeds); spans diseases. #> • research -- Research surveys/studies (household or community level); #>   DA-managed, flexible measure. #> • cmr -- (transitional, legacy reads only) Old CMR source token; new CMR writes #>   use `programmatic` + format: cmr. #> • odk -- (transitional, legacy reads only) Old ODK source token; new ODK writes #>   use `research` + format: odk. #>  #> ── data_type (the measure / what it captures) ── #>  #> • case -- Individual case records (one row per patient). #> • aggregate -- Aggregated counts (one row per place/period). #> • treatment -- MDA / treatment coverage (target & treated). #> • mmdp -- Morbidity management & disability prevention (e.g. LF #>   hydrocele/lymphoedema). #> • training -- Programmatic training activity (CDD / CS / MMDP). #> • survey -- Programmatic survey (examined / positive). #> • tas -- Transmission assessment survey (LF). #> • prevalence -- Prevalence survey (e.g. Kato-Katz, skin snip). #> • entomology -- Vector / entomological surveillance (e.g. larval prospection). #>  #> ── format (input shape of a programmatic source) ── #>  #> • cmr -- Country Monitoring Report Excel template (a programmatic input #>   format). #> • moh_feed -- A direct Ministry-of-Health data feed (a programmatic input #>   format that is not a CMR). #> • odk -- ODK Central survey instrument (a research collection format). #>  #> ── layer ── #>  #> "raw", "staged", and "processed"

what: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: Constructs canonical blob storage path following erifunctions five-axis data model (ADR-0012): {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/{layer}/, data_source channel (data arrives) data_type measure (captures). Use instead hard-coding path strings. legacy four-axis form eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, layer[, filename]) still accepted ADR-0012 migration builds measure-less {country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{layer}/ path — detected fourth argument layer keyword (data_type measure never ).

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
code: eri_data_path(country, disease, data_source, data_type, layer, filename = NULL)

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: country str Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht", "uga"). disease str Disease name (e.g. "malaria", "lf", "oncho"). data_source str channel: "surveillance", "programmatic", "research" (extensible — see eri_data_model(); unknown values warn). data_type str measure: "case", "aggregate", "treatment", "tas", ... (extensible; unknown values warn). layer str Pipeline layer: "raw", "staged", "processed". filename str Optional filename append. NULL (default), returns directory path .

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
text: character string canonical blob path.

title: Build a canonical blob path in the data/ container — eri_data_path
code: eri_data_path("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "case", "staged") #> [1] "dr/malaria/surveillance/case/staged" #> "dr/malaria/surveillance/case/staged"  eri_data_path("uga", "oncho", "programmatic", "treatment", "raw", "2024_06.parquet") #> [1] "uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/raw/2024_06.parquet" #> "uga/oncho/programmatic/treatment/raw/2024_06.parquet"

title: Convert a Date to a CDC epiweek number — eri_date_to_epiweek
text: integer vector epiweek numbers (1–53).

title: Convert a Date to a CDC epiweek number — eri_date_to_epiweek

what: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: general analyst ingest entry point. Reads raw local file, runs DQ checks via run_dq_checks(), prints flags, writes cleaned parquet data/{country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/staged/ — feeding eri_approve() matching measure. runs data, including throwaway sandbox: pipeline-registry country gate default. legacy projects-blob dual-write (hsp-mal cutover comparison) opt-mirror: pass mirror_pipeline = "hsp-mal" additionally mirror cleaned output projects/{project_folder}/intermediate/{country_subfolder}/. transitional removed Phase-3 cutover (ADR-0012).

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
code: eri_ingest(   path,   country,   disease,   data_source = "surveillance",   data_type = "aggregate",   schema = NULL,   data_con = NULL,   mirror_pipeline = NULL,   projects_con = NULL )

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: path str Local path raw file ingest. country str Country code (e.g. "dr", "ht"). disease str Disease name (e.g. "malaria"). data_source str channel ("surveillance", "programmatic", "research"). Default "surveillance". data_type str NULL measure (e.g. "aggregate", "case", "treatment"). Selects DQ schema measure level staged path .../{data_source}/{data_type}/staged/. Default "aggregate". Whatever pass , promote measure — eri_approve(country, disease, data_source, period, data_type = <>) — approve look one level find nothing. NULL stages channel-level (four-axis), rare measure-less case. schema Named list load_dq_schema(). NULL (default), loaded (country, disease, data_source, data_type). data_con Azure container data blob. NULL (default), connects using ERIFUNCTIONS_DATA_STORAGE_NAME. mirror_pipeline str NULL set (e.g. "hsp-mal"), also mirror cleaned output legacy projects blob via pipeline registry entry. Default NULL (mirror; sandbox-safe). projects_con Azure container projects blob; used mirror_pipeline set. NULL, connects automatically.

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
text: Invisibly, dq_result object ($data, $log, $flags).

title: Ingest a local data file: DQ-check and stage it — eri_ingest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default measure is "aggregate", so it stages to # dr/malaria/surveillance/aggregate/staged/ ... result <- eri_ingest("data/raw/dr_malaria_2024W01.xlsx", "dr", "malaria") result$flags  # review before approving # ... and the same measure promotes it: eri_approve("dr", "malaria", "surveillance", "2024W01", data_type = "aggregate") } # }

what: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs
title: List the operation / DQ log backlog for triage — eri_logs

Filter failures status = "error" data-quality items status = "needs_review", close items eri_logs_resolve().

text: country, disease, data_source chr NULL Scope search country, disease, channel; left NULL enumerated blob. data_type chr NULL Scope single measure (five-axis layout). Default FALSE. data_con Azure container data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

what: Permanently remove an ODK form from the shared Azure registry — eri_odk_purge
title: Permanently remove an ODK form from the shared Azure registry — eri_odk_purge
text: Hard-deletes every matching registry entry — active already soft-deleted — removing odk/registry.yaml entirely. Unlike eri_odk_deregister(), soft-deletes (active: false) preserves sync history, leaves trace. Use clean practice / sandbox registrations (otherwise linger inactive rows shared registry); real form prefer eri_odk_deregister(), keeps audit trail.

what: Sync an ODK form's submissions to Azure — eri_odk_sync
title: Sync an ODK form's submissions to Azure — eri_odk_sync
text: Downloads submissions registered ODK form writes Parquet file(s) data/{country}/{disease}/research/raw/ Azure data/ container — ODK research channel's collection format (format: odk) ADR-0012, data_source . measure (data_type) assigned later, analyst cleans form final dataset. Forms repeat groups (real forms) export multiple tables – main submission table plus one child table per repeat group – written Parquet ({form_id}.parquet, {form_id}-{repeat}.parquet, ...); flat form writes single {form_id}.parquet. registry entry's last_synced timestamp updated success.

title: Sync an ODK form's submissions to Azure — eri_odk_sync
code: eri_odk_sync(   project_id,   form_id,   con = NULL,   data_con = NULL,   overwrite = TRUE )

text: project_id int ODK Central project ID. form_id str ODK Central form ID (xmlFormId). con odk_connection NULL ODK connection init_odk_connection(). NULL, falls back ODK_URL ODK_TOKEN environment variables. data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically using ERIFUNCTIONS_* environment variables. overwrite lgl Whether overwrite existing Parquet file Azure. Defaults TRUE.

title: Sync an ODK form's submissions to Azure — eri_odk_sync
text: Invisibly, downloaded tibble (single-table forms) named list tibbles (forms repeat groups); invisible(NULL) zero submissions found.

what: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: inverse eri_odk_sync() / download_odk_form(): take table already-collected records (paper backfill, legacy export, download_odk_form() result) create submissions existing published ODK Central form. One submission POSTed per row. See ADR-0013 design contract.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
code: eri_odk_upload(   data,   project_id,   form_id,   con = NULL,   url = Sys.getenv("ODK_URL"),   auth = Sys.getenv("ODK_TOKEN"),   key_col = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   data_con = NULL )

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: data file path (CSV/Excel, flat forms ), data.frame, named list tables (parent first, "{form_id}-{repeat}" child tables) – download_odk_form()(tables = TRUE) shape. project_id int ODK Central project ID. form_id chr ODK Central form ID (xmlFormId); form must published. con odk_connection init_odk_connection(), NULL use ODK_URL / ODK_TOKEN environment variables. url, auth chr Server URL / bearer token, used con = NULL. key_col chr Column name(s) whose values seed deterministic instanceID. NULL (default) hashes whole parent row. preserve original submission identity round-trip, pass id column (e.g. "KEY"). dry_run lgl TRUE, run validation POST nothing; returns validation-issue tibble. data_con Azure container optional operation logging; NULL skips .

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Invisibly: dry_run = TRUE, validation tibble (table, column, row, issue); otherwise per-row outcome tibble (instance_id, status created/skipped/failed, http_status, message).

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Columns matched form fields name, using flattening download_odk_form() emits: field /data/visit/date column visit-date; repeat groups supplied separate child tables named "{form_id}-{repeat}" linked parent PARENT_KEY column whose value matches parent row's KEY (ADR-0010). download_odk_form(tables = TRUE) result therefore valid data argument – download/upload round-trips. submission's meta/instanceID derived deterministically key_col (whole row key_col NULL), re-running extract re-derives ids ODK Central rejects duplicates HTTP 409 (reported skipped) instead double-loading.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
text: Attachments attached submission creation (ODK API constraint) scope. Choice-list validation best-effort: values fields backed external/dataset choices checked surface failed rows POST time invalid. Submission XML built without instance namespace, matching XLSForm-generated forms.

title: Bulk-create ODK Central submissions from a tabular extract — eri_odk_upload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- init_odk_connection()  # Round-trip: pull, correct locally, push back. tabs <- download_odk_form(con = con, project_id = 7,                           form_id = "RiverProspection", tables = TRUE)  # Preview validation without sending anything. eri_odk_upload(tabs, project_id = 7, form_id = "RiverProspection",                con = con, key_col = "KEY", dry_run = TRUE)  # Create the submissions (re-runs skip already-present rows via HTTP 409). eri_odk_upload(tabs, project_id = 7, form_id = "RiverProspection",                con = con, key_col = "KEY") } # }

what: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
code: eri_research_pull(   country = NULL,   disease = NULL,   data_source = NULL,   data_type = NULL,   path = NULL,   dest = NULL,   data_con = NULL,   progress = FALSE )

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: country chr NULL Country code (e.g. "dr"). Used disease data_source. disease chr NULL Disease name (e.g. "malaria"). Used country data_source. data_source chr NULL channel ("surveillance", "programmatic", "research"). Used country disease. catalog (eri_catalog_query()) reports column, pull uses coordinates discovery query returns. data_type chr NULL measure (e.g. "case", "aggregate", "treatment", "tas"). Optional — NULL pulls four-axis channel-level processed layer. Back-compat: data_type given (pre-ADR-0012 form held channel), treated data_source. path chr NULL Explicit Azure blob path download . Mutually exclusive canonical args. dest chr NULL Local directory download files . Defaults data/ inside getwd(). data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically. progress lgl TRUE, show per-file byte progress bar (use large files, e.g. LandScan raster). Default FALSE uses one compact progress bar across files.

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: Character vector local file paths downloaded (invisibly).

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
text: Canonical: supply country, disease, data_source (optionally data_type measure) pull standard processed layer ({country}/{disease}/{data_source}/{data_type}/processed/, per ADR-0012). Path: supply path pull Azure location (e.g. "data/spatial/dom_admin_boundaries/"). non-standard external files yet Azure, upload first eri_artifact_upload(), pull eri_artifact_pull().

title: Pull data from Azure into a research project — eri_research_pull
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pull canonical processed data using the coordinates the catalog reports eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria",                   data_source = "surveillance", data_type = "case")  # Four-axis (channel only, no measure) eri_research_pull(country = "dr", disease = "malaria", data_source = "surveillance")  # Pull standard spatial reference files eri_research_pull(path = "spatial/dom_admin_boundaries") } # }

what: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote
title: Promote a research-project boundary to the canonical /spatial store — eri_spatial_promote
text: explicit gate pushing boundary cleaned research project shared canonical data/spatial/{country}/adm{level}.rds, users studies pull . Unlike eri_spatial_upload() (brand-new boundaries), eri_spatial_promote() deliberate way replace existing canonical boundary, records promotion project's research.yaml provenance. Replacing existing boundary still requires explicit overwrite = TRUE shared data never clobbered accident, prior canonical version first archived spatial/_archive/<timestamp>/ replacement reversible. See ADR-0009.

Default FALSE. path chr Local project root (read research.yaml record provenance). Defaults getwd(). research.yaml found, promotion proceeds recorded (warning). data_con Azure container object data/ blob. NULL, connects automatically.

what: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: thin, opt-data-sourcing helper maps messy, free-text locality names incoming data canonical admin units authoritative boundary sf object (eri_spatial_load()). name-reconciliation step many studies hand analysis – analysis tool (matching/windowing/modelling stay research repo; see ADR-0006).

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
code: eri_spatial_reconcile(   data,   loc_cols,   shapefile,   admin_cols,   country_name = NULL,   method = "osm",   max_dist = 0L,   status_col = "reconcile_status",   coord_cols = c("longitude", "latitude"),   ... )

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: data data frame tibble containing free-text place-name columns. loc_cols chr vector free-text column names, ordered finest coarsest (e.g. c("loc", "mun", "prov")). Used match build geocoding address (finest first). shapefile admin-boundary sf object, e.g. eri_spatial_load(). admin_cols chr vector canonical name columns shapefile, parallel loc_cols (length, finest--coarsest order). ordering load-bearing: passing columns coarsest-first silently produces wrong matches. country_name chr Country name appended geocoding address (e.g. "Dominican Republic"), improving geocoder accuracy. Optional. method chr Geocoding service passed tidygeocoder::geocode() (e.g. "osm", "google"). NULL disables geocoding (match-). Default "osm". max_dist int Maximum edit distance approximate match finest level. 0 (default) requires exact normalized match. status_col chr Name status column added result. Default "reconcile_status"; values "matched", "geocoded", "geocoded_review" (geocoded low-confidence parent-inconsistent – verify use), "unresolved". coord_cols chr length-2 names longitude latitude columns added result. Default c("longitude", "latitude"). Populated row sent geocoder, regardless final status (geocoded points fall outside every polygon still record coordinates). ... Passed tidygeocoder::geocode() (e.g. min_time, api_options).

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: tibble: data loc_cols replaced canonical values confidently reconciled (originals kept unresolved flagged "geocoded_review"), plus two coord_cols, status_col, one geocoded_<admin_col> column per admin_cols level. geocoded_* columns hold admin units geocoded point fell (point--polygon), filled every geocoded row landed inside polygon – including "geocoded_review" rows – can see flagged row disagreed (resolve ) without re-running spatial join . NA string-matched, unresolved, outside-polygon rows.

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
text: reconciliation runs two passes (per issue #134): Match first. Free-text names normalized (lower-cased, accent- punctuation-stripped, whitespace-squished) matched canonical names. Coarser levels must match exactly; finest level may match approximately max_dist > 0 (Levenshtein distance via utils::adist()). Matched rows geocoded. Geocode residual. Rows match geocoded address built loc_cols (+ country_name), assigned canonical admin unit point--polygon via eri_spatial_join(). Set method = NULL skip geocoding entirely (match-). geocode trusted (status "geocoded", names assigned) service flag partial/low-confidence match assigned coarser admin units agree parent levels supplied loc_cols. Otherwise row flagged "geocoded_review": coordinates recorded inspection analyst's names left untouched. guards geocoders "best-guess" fabricated unmatched locality plausible nearby point (issue #145). partial-match signal currently read "google" method; methods expose one rely parent-consistency check alone. place-name address strings sent geocoder; data records leave machine. "google" method accurate requires API key billed per call: sign key, store user .Renviron GOOGLEGEOCODE_API_KEY (e.g. via usethis::edit_r_environ()) restart R. function checks key front explains missing. default "osm" (Nominatim) needs key. See tidygeocoder::geocode().

title: Reconcile free-text place names to canonical admin units — eri_spatial_reconcile
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dr_loc <- eri_spatial_load("dr", level = 4, cache = TRUE) incidence <- eri_spatial_reconcile(   incidence,   loc_cols     = c("loc", "mun", "prov"),   shapefile    = dr_loc,   admin_cols   = c("adm4_name", "adm3_name", "adm2_name"),   country_name = "Dominican Republic",   method       = "google",  # needs GOOGLEGEOCODE_API_KEY   max_dist     = 1 ) table(incidence$reconcile_status) } # }

title: erifunctions i/o handler — erifunctions_io
text: Manages read/write/list/create/delete functions erifunctions. function adapted tidypolis_io. ergonomic interface tab-completion per-operation help pages, consider verb-named wrappers: eri_read(), eri_write(), eri_list(), eri_file_exists(), eri_dir_exists(), eri_dir_create(), eri_delete(), eri_dir_delete().

title: erifunctions i/o handler — erifunctions_io
text: io str type operation use. Valid values include: "read": reads data specified file_path. "write": writes data specified file_path. "list": lists files specified file_path. "exists.dir": determines whether directory present. "exists.file": determines whether file present. "create.dir": creates directory specified file_path. "delete": deletes file specified file_path. "delete.dir": deletes folder specified file_path. file_loc str Path file. obj str Object loaded Azure azure logical Whether function interact TCC Azure environment. Defaults TRUE, otherwise, interacts files locally. azcontainer Azure container container object returned get_azure_storage_connection(). full_names logical io="list", include full reference path. Default TRUE. progress logical Show AzureStor's byte progress bar transfer. Default FALSE (suppressed; erifunctions renders output). Set TRUE large single read/upload. ... Optional parameters work readr::read_delim() readxl::read_excel().

title: erifunctions i/o handler — erifunctions_io
text: Conditional io. io "read", return tibble. io "list", return list file names. Otherwise, function return NULL. exists.dir exists.file return logical.

title: Validate connection to Azure — get_azure_storage_connection
text: tenant_id str Azure tenant. Defaults ERIFUNCTIONS_TENANT_ID env var, TCC ERI Entra tenant unset. app_id str Application (client) ID. Defaults Sys.getenv("ERIFUNCTIONS_STORAGE_NAME"). auth str Authorization type defaults "authorization_code", can changed service principal. Valid values :"authorization_code", "device_code", "client_credentials", "resource_owner", "on_behalf_of". See Details AzureAuth::get_azure_token() details. creds_yaml_path str Path YAML credentials file containing service principal credentials (tcc_azure$client_id, tcc_azure$client_secret). NULL (default) environment variables ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_ID / ERIFUNCTIONS_SP_CLIENT_SECRET set, used automatically. Otherwise falls back interactive auth via auth. ... additional parameters passed AzureAuth::get_azure_token().
